E1.note: In the ocean, Alvida and her pirate crew attack a cruise ship. Coby, a slave of Alvida, discovers a barrel.

E2.note: Luffy and Coby arrive at a place with a large Marine base, where Luffy discovers the swordsman, Roronoa Zoro tied to a pole.

E3.note: Luffy hands the three swords to Zoro in exchange for Zoro joining his crew. Luffy and Morgan engage in battle. All of Morgan's attacks miss and Luffy knocks Morgan to the ground.

E4.note: In a flashback, a seven-year-old Luffy sits in a bar filled with the pirate crew of the "Red-Haired" Shanks, begging him to take him out to sea. But the pirate captain refuses.

E5.note: Nami pretends that Luffy is her boss, using the distraction to escape from Buggy's men. After Luffy beats them, she asks him to join her.

E6.note: Still trapped in the cage, Luffy meets a small dog named Chouchou guarding his master's store, despite knowing that his owner is dead.

E7.note: Enraged by the actions of Buggy's crew, the city's mayor Boodle rushes to fight Buggy himself, but the Straw Hat Pirates come to his rescue.

E8.note: Buggy reveals that Shanks accidentally caused him to eat the Devil Fruit he had been planning to sell while they were apprentice pirates on the same ship.

E9.note: When Luffy, Zoro, and Nami land on another island, Usopp appears to greet them, with three children hiding in the bushes.

E10.note: At Kaya's mansion, Klahadore goes on about how Usopp's father is a filthy pirate, making Usopp livid and punch him in the face.

E11.note: Zoro, Nami, and the Usopp Pirates still waiting around for Luffy and Usopp. The Usopp Pirates mention how Usopp always goes to the coast.

E12.note: Usopp, Luffy, Zoro, and Nami formulate a plan on the slope for when the pirates come. Meanwhile, Kuro breaks the glasses Kaya gave him and injures Merry with his clawed glove.

E13.note: Jango kneels beside the fallen pirates and starts to hypnotize them.

E14.note: The episode begins with Kuro standing impatiently. Siam and Butchie decide that Kuro, having been inactive for three years, has lost his edge, and that they can defeat him.

E15.note: Siam and Buchie (of the Meowban Brothers) face off once again against Zoro, as Luffy and Kuro begin their fight.

E16.note: The episode begins with the Usopp Pirates and Kaya running from Jango through the forest.

E17.note: Kuro's ultimate attack, "Shakushi", attacks everything in the area, whether friend or foe, and Luffy becomes enraged by his lack of regard for his crew.

E18.note: The episode opens with Usopp pretending he is in a great battle as captain of the Going Merry, fending off attacking pirates.

M1.note: One Piece: The Movie is a movie placed around Syrup Village. Watch after episode 18 (Usopp has joined; before Sanji).

E19.note: While Usopp & Luffy fool around with the ship's cannon one day, Zoro dreams about his childhood. He had joined a dojo after losing to the teacher's daughter, Kuina.

E20.note: After a review of the ending of the previous episode, the episode begins with Marine Lieutenant Iron Fist Fullbody pouring a drink to a beautiful lady on his marine ship.

E21.note: After a review of the last episode, the episode begins with Patty, a cook for Baratie, picking his nose in the bathroom.

E22.note: Ghin returns to the Baratie with his captain, Don Krieg, on a nearly destroyed ship, and begs the cooks to feed him.

E23.note: It is revealed that Chef Zeff was a great pirate known as Red Foot Zeff who sailed in the Grand Line, but lost his leg at some point.

E24.note: As Nami steals the Going Merry, Mihawk has returned to finish the job, but Zoro challenges him to a duel in order to achieve his dream of being the world's strongest swordsman.

E25.note: Mihawk leaves after defeating Zoro, so Krieg continues his invasion.

E26.note: With Zeff held captive by Ghin, Sanji has no choice but to take Pearl's hits.

E27.note: Ghin finishes off Pearl, claiming that he wants to kill Sanji himself. A lengthy battle ensues but Ghin eventually gains the upper hand after Sanji breaks his ribs.

E28.note: It is revealed that the mask Luffy was wearing was Ghin's while he held Sanji's to his face.

E29.note: Even after taking several hits from the Battle Spear, Luffy refuses to give up.

E30.note: After the battle, Ghin decides to go back to the Grand Line with Krieg despite still being poisoned, and Sanji makes the difficult decision on whether to stay or to go.

E31.note: The crew chases after Nami, who stole their ship, and Yosaku takes time during the trip to explain about Arlong and his Fishmen to Luffy and Sanji.

E32.note: Zoro is captured by the fishmen after Usopp and Johnny ditch him, and it's revealed that Nami was part of Arlong's crew the entire time.

E33.note: After Zoro escapes with Nami's help, she tries to prove her loyalty to the Arlong Pirates by appearing to kill Usopp. Meanwhile, Luffy's group finally arrives at the island.

E34.note: Johnny tells the others about Nami killing Usopp, and Luffy doesn't believe them until Nami appears herself and confirms it.

E35.note: Nojiko starts her story about Nami's childhood and their adopted mother, Bellemere, a former Marine.

E36.note: Nojiko continues her story, explaining how their life was ruined when the Arlong Pirates first took over the village.

E37.note: When a marine officer under Arlong's pay confiscates all the money Nami stole over the years, in order to one day buy the village from Arlong.

E38.note: The Straw Hats face the Fishmen themselves as the villagers watch from the sidelines.

E39.note: Arlong has trapped Luffy in the ocean and Zoro faces the six-sword–wielding octopus Hatchan.

E40.note: With Hatchan defeated, two of the fishmen battle Sanji and Usopp.

E41.note: Arlong's officers have been defeated, but Sanji and Zoro prove to be no match for Arlong in their current state.

E42.note: Luffy returns to the fight, and the battle with Arlong versus Luffy begins.

E43.note: The battle spreads into Arlong Park itself and Arlong is no longer holding back. Luffy realizes Nami was forced to draw charts for Arlong and becomes enraged.

E44.note: Arlong's reign of terror is over, and no one has anything on their mind except to party.

R3.note: One Piece: Episode of Nami - Tears of a Navigator and the Bonds of Friends revisits earlier story material and is placed around Arlong Park.

R6.note: One Piece: Episode of East Blue - Luffy and His Four Crewmates' Great Adventure revisits earlier story material and is placed around Arlong Park.

E45.note: As a result of defeating Arlong as well as defeating Don Krieg and Buggy, Luffy now has the highest bounty in East Blue at 30,000,000 berries.

E46.note: After his fight with Luffy, Buggy was sent flying to another island with several body parts missing.

E47.note: With Buggy assumed to be dead, his crew fights over who will be the new captain, only to be captured by a tribe of cannibals.

E48.note: The crew arrives at the last large town before the entrance to the Grand Line: Loguetown, the place where Gold Roger was both born and executed. They stock up on supplies and sight-see.

E49.note: While searching for new swords to replace the ones shattered by Mihawk, Zoro meets a woman named Tashigi, a marine Sergeant Major with a passion for swords.

E50.note: Usopp manages to anger the daughter of bounty hunter Daddy the Father, and finds his own life at stake when he challenges Daddy to a duel, which he proceeds to lose.

E51.note: Sanji enters a cooking contest against a woman named Five-Alarm Carmen for a fish that he has always dreamed of cooking. Meanwhile, Buggy and Alvida show up and capture Luffy.

E52.note: Zoro and Sanji show up too late to stop Buggy but a bolt of lightning shows up just in time to cancel the execution.

E53.note: Miraculously saved from death by an unnatural green gust and a lightning bolt to Buggy, the Straw Hats rush out of Loguetown, only for Zoro to be stopped by Tashigi.

SP1.note: One Piece: Adventure in the Ocean's Navel is a TV special placed around Loguetown. Watch after episode 53 / before Warship Island or Reverse Mountain.

E54.note: On a stormy night, a girl called Apis escapes from the Marines and is found drifting by the Straw hats.

E55.note: The Calm Belt is revealed to have no winds or currents as well as being the home of many giant sea kings.

E56.note: Now that the crew understand why Apis was followed, they hurry to escape the island so Ryuuji can be saved, but they meet with Erik and the Marines.

E57.note: After arriving at what seemed to be Lost Island, the Straw Hats find an old Temple with paintings that might indicate where the Lost Island really is.

E58.note: Zoro tries to fight Erik, but Erik is more interested in chasing the dragon. Zoro manages to trip him up long enough to the rest to make it to the ship.

E59.note: Admiral Nelson, who never fully trusted Erik, has mobilized the full extent of the 8th fleet.

E60.note: Luffy talks to Ryuuji in his final moments and comes to understand just what is going on.

E61.note: Apis is saddened by the death of Ryuuji but Luffy explains that the dragons come back to Lost Island be reborn. Nearby, an egg cracks open and a young dragon with Ryuuji's eyes emerges.

M2.note: One Piece: Clockwork Island Adventure is a movie placed around Warship Island. Watch after episode 61 (East Blue crew plus Sanji).

S1.note: One Piece: Django's Dance Carnival is a short extra placed around Warship Island. Short bundled with Movie 2; watch after episode 61.

E62.note: As the Straw Hats ride down Reverse Mountain into the Grand Line, a huge whale appears in front of them.

E63.note: While guiding them out of the giant whale Laboon, its caretaker, Crocus, tells the whale's story to the Straw Hats, that it was left behind by a group of pirates.

E64.note: The weather changes rapidly on the first leg of their journey, from Reverse Mountain to Whiskey Peak. Nami has to adjust to the thought of having to watch the log pose constantly.

E65.note: Nami who faked falling asleep as well, searches the town for the bounty hunters' treasures.

E66.note: Mr. 5 and Miss Valentine follow after Vivi, who attempts to flee the island. Mr. 9 despite learning Vivi's a traitor tries to protect her but is beaten.

E67.note: Luffy and Zoro are about to go all out, when Nami arrives and violently ends their duel.

E68.note: Coby and Helmeppo have enlisted into the navy as odd-jobbers. Coby works hard towards his goal of becoming an officer.

E69.note: Coby and Helmeppo settle in at headquarters, where Garp makes sure that they are not slacking off.

E70.note: As the Straw Hats sail toward Little Garden, the Baroque Works officer agents Mr. 3 and his partner Miss Golden Week receive new orders.

E71.note: The giant introduces himself as Broggy, claiming to be the strongest warrior of Elbaf, and asks Nami and Usopp for booze. Frightened, the two play dead.

E72.note: Usopp, who is immensely impressed with the giants' fight, decides to strive to be like them and starts calling Broggy his master.

E73.note: Before he leaves to face his rival and friend for a last time, Dorry traps Luffy under a huge rock.

E74.note: After explosively beating down on the still trapped Luffy, Mr. 5 and his partner take Vivi to the giants' duel site, where Mr. 3 is busy further restraining Broggy with his wax.

E75.note: Mr. 3 is unsettled, that Zoro and Nami seem to be completely carefree since Luffy's arrival. Usopp and Karoo face Mr.

E76.note: Once back at the pedestal, Usopp finds Luffy drinking tea with Miss Golden Week, while their friends are already completely covered in wax.

E77.note: Once Sanji realizes that he is talking to Crocodile, he pretends being Mr. 3 and convinces the Warlord that he has eliminated Vivi and her Straw Hat escort.

E78.note: Nami comes down with a terrible fever and the Straw Hats don’t know what to do. Looks like the Grand Line might claim another victim.

E79.note: While looking for a doctor, the crew meets the pirate Wapol, who attacks them while trying to eat their ship with his Munch-Munch Fruit power, but only makes Luffy send him flying.

E80.note: The Straw Hat crew manages to gain the trust of the people that attacked them, and are taken to the village, except for Zoro and Carue, who guard the ship.

E81.note: While Sanji and Luffy fight off the wilderness, the islander's worst nightmare turns true, as Wapol returns to the island.

E82.note: Dalton faces Wapol, but only gets himself hit by arrows thrown by his former comrade Chess. An avalanche stops the battle, but injures Sanji.

E83.note: The Lapahn help Luffy escape from Wapol, giving him enough time to climb to the castle where doctor Kureha lives. Vivi, Usopp and Zoro deal with the king's army in order to save Dalton.

E84.note: Nami asks Chopper to join the crew, but he isn't interested. Kureha tells Nami that Chopper is a reindeer who ate the Human-Human Fruit and some of what was his background.

E85.note: Doctorine continues to tell how Chopper lived his life and how the quack doctor Hiruluk took care of him.

E86.note: More of Chopper's past is revealed, including the fate of doctor Hiruluk and Wapol's cruelties as the king. Meanwhile, Wapol reaches the castle where the crew is residing.

E87.note: Luffy unleashes the full force of his Gum Gum fury on Wapol, and Chopper is amazed by the rubberman’s fierce devotion to the pirate flag.

E88.note: Chopper demonstrates the abilities of Rumble Balls when fighting Chessmarimo. Dalton is uncovered from the avalanche.

E89.note: After trying every trick in the castle, Wapol is finally hunted down by Luffy. The villagers along with Zoro, Vivi and Usopp start their ascent to the castle through a ropeway.

E90.note: After finally being convinced to join the team, Chopper announces his intentions to Dr.

E91.note: Chopper joins the Straw Hat crew as they continue their journey to Vivi's homeland.

M3.note: One Piece: Chopper Kingdom of Strange Animal Island is a movie placed around Drum Island. Watch after episode 91 (Chopper has joined).

S2.note: One Piece: Dream Soccer King is a short extra placed around Drum Island. Short bundled with Movie 3; watch after episode 91.

E92.note: Crocodile appears to stop rampaging pirates. Luffy and his crew meet Mr. 2 (Bon Clay) who shows off his devil fruit power and Vivi sees a shocking sight.

E93.note: Luffy separates from the rest of the crew, right after arriving at Alabasta's port city of Nanohana. The crew dons disguises while Luffy learns of Dance Powder.

E94.note: While in Nanohana, the Straw Hats encounter Smoker and Tashigi. A powerful pirate named Portgas D. Ace appears, and is revealed to be Luffy's older brother.

E95.note: Ace fights Smoker while the Straw Hats run for their ship.

E96.note: Ace decides to travel with the Straw Hats, as he is hunting for a man known as Blackbeard.

E97.note: After leaving Erumalu, the Straw Hats plus Vivi & Ace continue through the desert, facing the many dangers of it.

E98.note: Nami and Vivi found themselves an easier way to travel in the desert and leave all men behind. While trying to catch up with them, Ace gets lost.

E99.note: After hearing that a small village has fake Rebel Army bodyguards that have been keeping peace, Vivi asks the men of the crew to test if they're worthy of staying.

E100.note: Still trekking through the Alabasta desert, the crew beats up a giant desert scorpion for lunch, while Vivi tells Nami about her past and her friendship with Kohza.

E101.note: Ace fights against a bounty hunter, who claims to have defeated Blackbeard, although this was a lie to lure Ace out and challenge him.

E102.note: Zoro, Chopper and Luffy manage to get themselves lost and stumble across some huge underground ruins.

E103.note: Baroque Works plan starts to take effect, and for that, the Number Agents gather to meet Mr. 0.

E104.note: In Yuba, Luffy learns that Toto was entrusted by Cobra to watch over the land and figures out a way to stop the sandstorm.

E105.note: After the struggle, Vivi decides to go after Crocodile with the Straw Hats. The crew travels to the city of Rainbase, but run afoul of Smoker and Tashigi.

E106.note: Most of the Straw Hats, along with Smoker, are trapped by Crocodile. Vivi reunites with the royal guardian Pell, but is captured by Miss All-Sunday.

E107.note: Baroque Works' plan to take over Alabasta is set in motion. The king is kidnapped, and Mr.

E108.note: Most of the Straw Hats are helplessly trapped as Vivi tries desperately to get the key to their cage. but fails. Crocodile is contacted by someone known as "Mr. Prince.".

E109.note: "Mr Prince" is actually Sanji, and lures Crocodile out of the casino. Sanji goes to rescue the Straw Hats, and manages to open their cage by having Mr.

E110.note: After escaping from Rain Dinners, the Straw Hats and Vivi cross the desert to Alubarne, where the final battle will take place.

E111.note: Luffy is left for dead by Crocodile, but is helped by the mysterious Miss All-Sunday. The rest of the Straw Hats reach Alubarna.

E112.note: The revolution is on the verge of starting and the crew comes out with a plan that could stop it.

E113.note: Karoo musters up the strength to carry Vivi up Alubarna's walls and into the city. When Karoo collapses, Mr. 2 is attacked by Sanji and members of the supersonic duck force.

E114.note: Usopp & Chopper struggle against the Mr. 4 team's attacks, with Miss Merry Christmas attacking from underground and Mr. 4 batting explosive balls.

E115.note: Usopp is badly injured by a combination attack by the Mr. 4 team, but with Chopper's help, he tricks Mr.

E116.note: Sanji manages to figure out that Mr. 2 must transform into his original form to use his Ballet Kenpo, and finally manages to defeat him.

E117.note: Nami faces off against Miss Doublefinger and tries to figure out her new weapon, the Clima Takt (Climate Baton), at the same time.

E118.note: While Crocodile reveals to Vivi that he seeks the ancient weapon Pluton, Nami manages to defeat Miss Doublefinger with Tornado Tempo.

E119.note: Zoro struggles against Mr. 1 as his attacks have no effect on his steel body, but Mr. 1's Dice Dice Fruit enables him to attack by using any part of his body as a steel blade. After Mr.

E120.note: When Vivi reveals Crocodile's plan to Koza, he attempts to stop the fighting, but is shot. Crocodile reveals that he has set up a massive bomb in the city.

E121.note: As Koza tries to stop the Rebel Army, both sides are once more fighting due to the intervention of Baroque Works.

E122.note: Luffy fights Crocodile with more success this time by using water, but is defeated after Crocodile dries up the ground.

E123.note: Crocodile joins Robin in the tomb, and orders her to translate the Poneglyph.

E124.note: Luffy fights with Crocodile by using blood on his fists, but Crocodile reveals that he has a poisonous hook, and plans on finishing Luffy off once and for all.

E125.note: Vivi finds the bomb inside of the clock tower, but the Mr. 7 pair of Baroque Works are there to protect it. Vivi defeats them, but she realizes the bomb cannot be stopped so easily.

E126.note: Luffy defeats Crocodile by punching him through solid bedrock. When Crocodile falls, the rain-drops calm the soldiers.

E127.note: Despite the rain falling, some rebels still wish to fight, but Igaram appears with proof of Cobra's innocence, as well as Koza telling everything.

E128.note: Luffy is rescued from the collapsed tomb where he fought Crocodile and brought into Cobra's palace to rest.

E129.note: Bon Clay and his crew attempt to protect the Straw Hats from Hina, and sacrifice themselves to serve as a diversion.

E130.note: The Marines fail again to catch the Straw Hats. After escaping from the Marines, Nico Robin (Ms. All Sunday) appears from inside of the Straw Hats ship.

M4.note: One Piece: Dead End Adventure is a movie placed around Post-Arabasta. Watch after episode 130 (Robin has joined).

R1.note: One Piece: Episode of Alabasta - The Desert Princess and the Pirates revisits earlier story material and is placed around Arabasta. Alabasta recap movie; placed after the Arabasta arc.

E131.note: The Straw Hats land on an uninhabited island; Sanji immediately notices that it is covered with fruit-bearing trees. The crew decides to divide the work by drawing straws.

E132.note: While fishing with Chopper and Usopp, Luffy fishes an ammonite-shaped vehicle out of the sea.

E133.note: In a thick bank of fog, the Straw Hats run into a fleet of navy ships.

E134.note: Because it is raining, Luffy reads out of boredom. Fortunately for him, the Straw Hats' ship reaches the next island.

E135.note: Before he meets Luffy, Zoro traveled the world in search of "Hawk-Eye" Mihawk. In a flashback, Zoro captures the target of the bounty hunters Johnny and Yosaku in a bar.

E136.note: A few navy ships chase the Straw Hats.

E137.note: The Straw Hats continue to do Zenny's daily work and finish building his pirate ship, which he intends to use as a coffin.

E138.note: The commotion caused by Zenny's arrival allows Nami, Sanji, Usopp, and Chopper to board their ship safely.

E139.note: After escaping from another fleet of navy ships, the Straw Hats drop anchor outside a town with a huge tower.

E140.note: Inside the mist, Luffy and his group discover a large ship graveyard.

E141.note: After entering the Rainbow Mist, the children followed the orders of Ian, the only pirate left on the ship.

E142.note: Lapanui accuses Henzo of being Whetton's lapdog and says that in the past, Henzo would have never allowed that to happen.

E143.note: Luffy and Lapanui are outside the mist and use the newly created bridge to re-enter it.

E144.note: Nami's log pose begins to point to the sky, after that, a big ship falls from sky and the crew finds a map in of an island named 'Skypiea' on a 200-year-old ship.

E145.note: The Straw Hats decide to go to Jaya island to look for information on Skypeia.

E146.note: Jaya's main town is full of famous pirates that are constantly brawling with each other.

E147.note: Luffy, Zoro, and Nami meet the man who is later revealed to be Blackbeard. They leave Mock Town, and get into a fight with Masira's brother Shoujo, another salvage monkey.

E148.note: The Straw Hats meet with Montblanc Cricket on another part of Jaya; Cricket is a descendant of Montblanc Noland, an infamous "liar" who told of a gold city on Jaya.

E149.note: Cricket explains how the Straw Hats can ride a dangerous vertical current called the Knock-Up Stream to get to Skypeia.

SP2.note: One Piece: Open Upon the Great Sea! A Father's Huge, HUGE Dream! is a TV special placed around Jaya. Aired between episodes 149 and 150.

E150.note: While the Straw Hats are looking for a Southbird, Bellamy and his crew attack Cricket's house, and steal the gold artifacts he'd collected over the years from his salvage work.

E151.note: Luffy takes out Bellamy with a single punch. Meanwhile, the world government heads are concerned over Whitebeard, Shanks, and Luffy.

E152.note: The Going Merry is refitted by Masira and Shoujo to be more flight capable, and the Straw Hats catch a ride on the Knock-Up Stream for Skypeia.

E153.note: The Straw Hats arrive in an ocean above the clouds. Usopp almost falls out of the sky island but is saved at the last minute.

E154.note: As the crew arrives in Skypiea, they're greeted by Conis and her pet Suu. Both she and her father start explaining how the life in Skypiea is and how the different Dials work.

E155.note: Nami finds out what should happen to anyone who enters the forest.

E156.note: The Straw Hats have some problems with the local justice enforcer force called "The White Berets.".

E157.note: Before they can leave Chopper, Robin, Nami and Zoro are abducted by a giant shrimp. Luffy, Sanji and Usopp go after them to the upper yard.

E158.note: Conis leads Luffy, Usopp, and Sanji to a boat to get to Upper Yard, but she tells them it is a trap and almost gets hit by "Heaven's Judgment".

E159.note: Luffy, Usopp and Sanji finally enter Upper Yard, only to find out that the whole road is trapped heavily, but at last they arrive at the Ordeal Gates.

E160.note: Luffy, Sanji, and Usopp enter the Ordeal of Balls gate and encounter the Priest Satori.

E161.note: Usopp, Sanji, and Luffy fight Satori, but the power of Mantra seems to be too strong.

E162.note: Chopper, threatened by priest Shura, blows the whistle for Gan Fall to come, but before he can arrive, Priest Shura assaults Chopper and begins destroying the Going Merry.

E163.note: Satori is defeated by Luffy and Sanji. Meanwhile Gan Fall has been defeated and Chopper jumps in the water to rescue him.

E164.note: Chopper is saved by a bunch of giant Southbirds, who tell him that Gan Fall was actually God.

E165.note: The Shandians' attack on Upper Yard has started. The crew is finally reunited, and everyone speaks up about what has happened.

E166.note: After befriending a wolf pack, Gan Fall tells the crew a little more about the history of Upper Yard.

E167.note: Eneru takes away the limitations of the priests. Also, Usopp witnesses a mysterious person fixing the ship; the next day Merry is found to be back to her original form.

E168.note: A giant python makes Luffy, Robin, Chopper and Zoro split while going to the ruins at the south.

E169.note: Wyper takes out Shura and tells his goal to the Shandians, and Eneru speaks up about how many people will be alive by the end of his plan.

E170.note: Zoro fights the Shandian warrior Braham, and defeats him with One Sword Style: 36 Caliber Phoenix technique. ("36-Pound Cannon" In Japanese).

E171.note: Luffy fights Wyper, but after some time, he falls into some underground ruins.

E172.note: Chopper is attacked by Gedatsu, who turns out to be a complete moron, and eventually defeats him, sending him plummeting to the Blue Sea.

E173.note: Eneru defeats several members of the Shandian Tribe. Meanwhile, Gan Fall and Nami start and finish their fight with Kotori and Hotori.

E174.note: While Robin is looking at some ruins she found, Yama, The Chief Enforcer, attacks her. Yama is easily defeated by Robin. Later, she discovers the legendary city, Shandora.

SP3.note: One Piece: Protect! The Last Great Performance is a TV special placed around Skypiea. Aired between episodes 174 and 175.

E175.note: Priest Ohm appears and starts fighting everyone.

E176.note: At the Upper Ruins, the fight between Ohm, Zoro, Gan Fall and Wyper begins. When Nami appears, she is swallowed by the giant python, along with Gan Fall and Aisa.

E177.note: Ohm makes matters worse, creating a barbed wire cage around the area. Raki is knocked out by Eneru, and Nami, Aisa, and Gan Fall find out why the python was rampaging so much.

E178.note: Zoro finally defeats Priest Ohm with his 108 Caliber Phoenix technique. Meanwhile Pagaya and Conis hear the terrible truth about the goal of Eneru: to eradicate every person in the sky.

E179.note: Eneru appears only to reduce the group as close to his prediction as he can.

E180.note: Eneru counterattacks Gan Fall and lands a finishing hit. He then knocks out Robin as she tries to manipulate him.

E181.note: Eneru defeats Zoro and Wyper. Upon seeing this, Nami follows him to a cave where his ark, Maxim, is.

E182.note: Luffy and Eneru start fighting. Conis tells the dangers of staying in Sky Island to the other Skypieans.

E183.note: Eneru starts a machine that would bring the death of Sky Island: Deathpiea.

E184.note: Luffy falls from the ark. Nami starts fighting Eneru while Sanji and Usopp try to rescue her.

E185.note: Usopp and Sanji split up to rescue Nami, but the first one to get on deck is Usopp.

E186.note: Eneru's thunderclouds are coming down to Sky Island while the evacuation is half complete.

E187.note: The history of Noland and Calgara is told, as a flashback from Wyper.

E188.note: Noland and Calgara are enjoying themselves, as well as the villagers and Noland's crew, but that changes one day, as the villagers start hating their new friends.

E189.note: The cause of the hatred is discovered and explained. Meanwhile, Calgara vows to meet Noland again.

E190.note: Angel Island is destroyed by Eneru's attacks. Luffy decides to go and ring the golden bell before Eneru takes it away.

E191.note: Nami finds out a way to get to Eneru and sends a message to the others for them to cut down Giant Jack.

E192.note: Luffy defeats Eneru by smashing him into the Golden Bell, ringing it in the process.

E193.note: Luffy has won and the 400 year war on Sky Island is finally over.

E194.note: Robin deciphers the poneglyphs on the Golden Bell and realizes how to find the Rio Poneglyph. She also discovers that the poneglyphs on the Bell were written by Gol D.

E195.note: The Golden Bell rings as the Straw Hats are going down to the sea, meaning that they are welcome and are deeply thankful for what they did.

M5.note: One Piece: The Curse of the Sacred Sword is a movie placed around Skypiea. Watch after episode 195 / before G-8 if following release-era placement.

S3.note: One Piece: Take Aim! The Pirate Baseball King is a short extra placed around Skypiea. Short bundled with Movie 5; watch after episode 195.

R7.note: One Piece: Episode of Skypiea revisits earlier story material and is placed around Skypiea. Skypiea recap special; placed after Skypiea.

E196.note: The Marines are convinced the Going Merry is a ghost ship after it crashes inside their fortress from above. The crew members are scattered around the premises.

E197.note: In disguise, Sanji shows the Marine chefs the proper way to prepare a meal.

E198.note: Zoro is taken prisoner as Chopper and Nami assist a nervous doctor in treating injured Marines.

E199.note: Robin and Usopp both disguise themselves as the same visiting inspector, and Usopp is thrown in the brig with Zoro. Luffy and Sanji are discovered by Jonathan after a slight mishap.

E200.note: The real Inspector is thrown in the brig with Zoro and Usopp, while Sanji and Luffy race to rescue the two while avoiding detection.

E201.note: Sanji and Luffy, after freeing Usopp and Zoro, try to escape Navarone. As the Burning Soul combat unit is deployed, Dr. Kobato leads Nami and Chopper to the docks.

E202.note: The crew meets at the docks and are surrounded by Marines. Using Nami as their fake hostage, they trick the Marines into letting them back on the Going Merry.

E203.note: The crew disguises the Going Merry as a Marine ship so they can get their treasure back. With the gold, they decide they'll hire a shipwright.

E204.note: Usopp splits up the crew into two teams: the gold retrieval team and the waver retrieval team. Inspector Shepherd decides to catch Luffy on his own.

E205.note: Luffy, Zoro, Usopp, and Robin continue to search for their treasure. Luffy leads Nami to the captain's quarters where they retrieve their gold.

E206.note: The Going Merry is grounded during a period of low tide and Jonathan believes they've reached "checkmate".

E207.note: Luffy and company come across a mysterious pirate ship after trying to escape a giant wave. Soon, they arrive on an island inhabited by strange animals.

E208.note: After Tonjit re-unites with his horse, they are soon attacked by Captain Foxy and his subordinates, who challenge Luffy in a Davy Back Fight.

E209.note: The first round of Davy Back Fight involves a race around the island with tiny boats. Zoro's and Chopper's boat is eliminated.

E210.note: With only Luffy, Nami, and Porche's boat remaining, it's an all out war to the goal, but after Foxy exposes his Devil Fruit powers, Porche wins and takes Tony Tony Chopper from Luffy.

E211.note: Without Chopper, Sanji and Zoro must face the Foxy Pirates in a ball game, but are hampered by their blatant cheating and the crooked referee.

E212.note: The bickering rivals Sanji and Zoro manage to overcome the opposing team by working together. Luffy chooses Tonjit's horse, Sherry over Chopper.

E213.note: The Straw Hats face Foxy in a race around a giant ring on roller skates.

E214.note: Thanks to some quick planning, the Straw Hats win the race.

E215.note: With Luffy's victory, he takes Chopper back, but foolishly accepts another challenge by Foxy.

E216.note: Foxy decides to take Robin from Luffy this time because of her Devil Fruit ability. The next match is a race to the top of the island.

E217.note: With Chopper gone again from Luffy's crew, Luffy and Foxy face off in the final game: a battle of brawn aboard Foxy's ship.

E218.note: Luffy continues to battle Foxy with the odds not in his favor, and the fight continues inside Foxy's ship.

E219.note: Luffy manages to finally defeat Foxy by using his ability against him. After dismissing his new crew members, the Straw Hats leave in search for a shipwright.

E220.note: A mysterious figure appears during the night and steals the memories of the crew. In the morning, Robin is the only one who remembers the Straw Hats adventures together.

E221.note: Robin suggests that only those who were sleeping lost their memories. Luffy and the others then remember seeing the mysterious boy who played some kind of flute.

E222.note: During the night, Usopp, Chopper, and Sanji question what brought them to the Going Merry.

E223.note: On their way to "The Palace of the Sea God" Robin's group meets an entranced Zoro. It looks like Zoro wants to fight so Luffy urges the others to keep going without him.

E224.note: Luffy returns and knocks some sense into Zoro. Cornered, the sea horse spreads illusions of the people in their memories to distract them.

M6.note: One Piece: Baron Omatsuri and the Secret Island is a movie placed around Ocean's Dream. Watch after episode 224 (before the main Water 7 conflict).

E225.note: Luffy rescues Foxy, Hamburg, and Porche from a storm at sea. When they reunite with the Sexy Foxy ship, they find it captained by somebody new.

E226.note: Once Foxy wins back his crew, he captures the Straw Hats. Nami and Luffy fight Foxy and although he's defeated, his crew accepts him back.

M7.note: One Piece: The Giant Mechanical Soldier of Karakuri Castle is a movie placed around Foxy's Return. Watch after episode 226 (before Water 7).

E227.note: The man they met, Marine High Admiral Aokiji, appears to be incredibly lazy.

E228.note: Robin is frozen by Aokiji and Luffy tells the others to escape with her. In the Going Merry, Usopp and Chopper try to thaw her in the shower.

E229.note: After the Going Merry narrowly escapes being hit by a "sea train" which knocks away the giant frog attempting to derail it, the Straw Hats meet the stationmaster Kokoro along.

E230.note: Luffy, Nami, and Usopp get strange greetings from the residents and they get to experience the roller coaster-like channels of Water Seven.

E231.note: Zoro awakens and drives off the "Franky Family", a gang of hoodlum pirate hunters. Luffy's group gets the gold they acquired from Skypeia exchanged for 300,000,000 berries.

E232.note: The Franky Family nearly escape with the Straw Hats' money were it not for untimely intervention of Galley La's Paulie while he was evading debt collectors.

E233.note: Luffy refuses to believe that Galley La cannot repair the Going Merry before he and Nami realize that not only is Usopp missing now.

E234.note: Luffy, Sanji, Zoro, and Chopper follow Usopp's bloody trail to the Franky House, where they find him passed out in front.

E235.note: Once they're back on the ship, Luffy explains to a guilt-ridden Usopp that the Going Merry can no longer be repaired and has chosen to get a new ship instead.

E236.note: Usopp fights Luffy in order to decide who keeps the Going Merry; going all out while keeping a hesitant Luffy on his toes as he and others remember their time with Usopp.

E237.note: The Straw Hats get themselves a hotel room as Nami finds the crew on the roof and informs them that Iceberg has been shot.

E238.note: In the streets outside of the hospital, Luffy fights Franky as the latter reveals himself to be a cyborg.

E239.note: The Galley La Company continues to fight Luffy with an enraged Franky attempting to take both sides out.

E240.note: In a bar, while recharging his soda reserves, Franky talks with Kokoro about her theory that Iceberg was shot by an agent of World Government's shadow organization CP9.

E241.note: Chopper reunites with the others without Sanji and they discuss what's happened. Luffy decides that they need to catch Robin to know the full truth.

E242.note: The Straw Hats rush to Galley La as three CP9 agents commence their raid.

E243.note: Robin speaks with Iceberg while the masked man leaves to keep anyone from interfering, Robin learning that Iceberg possesses the blueprints to the ultimate warship, Pluton.

E244.note: Lucci tells Iceberg their hypothesis from learning of the decoy blueprints and has Kaku check Iceberg's pulse to confirm that he gave the blueprints to another.

E245.note: Luffy and the others request that Robin comes back to the crew. She refuses and tells them that she cannot accomplish her "wish" if she were to stay with them.

E246.note: Lucci explains he acquired the power of a Zoan Devil's Fruit; the Cat-Cat Fruit: Leopard-Type, before quickly disposes of Luffy and Zoro while CP9 tie up Paulie and Iceberg.

E247.note: Usopp believes that the person who repaired the Going Merry in Skypeia was really the ship itself, explaining Franky explaining that the ship was inhabited.

E248.note: The CP9 agents overpower Franky and discover a hidden room that they tear through to Franky's dismay of them trampling his memories.

E249.note: Eight years prior to the Straw Hats reaching Water Seven, a CP5 agent named Spandam seeks to acquire the Pluton blueprints from Tom.

E250.note: After telling Franky to take pride in the ships he created, Tom takes a swing at Spandam before confessing to the attack.

E251.note: Chopper escapes the burning building with Paulie and Iceberg on his back and collapses outside.

E252.note: Paulie helps clear the name of the Straw Hats but doesn't tell the mob everything he knows. He gets the mob to follow Chopper so they can search for the rest of the crew.

E253.note: Nami and the conductors are rescued by Paulie when the Aqua Laguna floods the main station. A man hands Nami a letter he found from Sanji.

SP4.note: One Piece: The Detective Memoirs of Chief Straw Hat Luffy is a TV special placed around Water 7. Historical/alternate setting special; release-era placement around Water 7.

E254.note: Nami finally reaches Luffy and tells him that he needs to hurry before Robin reaches Enies Lobby. Chopper finds Zoro and hands him the Sandai Kitetsu so he can escape the chimney.

E255.note: Sanji fights his way through another train car and finds Usopp and Franky.

E256.note: The Rocket Man finally makes it out to sea. As the train connects with the tracks, Luffy has to rescue Chimney before she's thrown off by the wind.

E257.note: Luffy and Zoro perform a move that acts similar to a cannon and blasts a hole through the wall of the tidal wave ahead of them, opening up a channel for them to pass through.

E258.note: Sogeking makes his grand entrance. Franky and Sanji aren't fooled by his costume but they try to humor him.

E259.note: Kalifa updates the CP9 on the situation with Franky, Sanji, and Usopp. Sanji tells Franky and Usopp to go on without him so he can fight Wanze and his "Ramen Kenpo".

E260.note: Franky's fight with Nero continues. Nero discovers that Franky's weak spot is his back, so Franky lies down flat so he can't get to it.

E261.note: Zoro clashes with "Ship Cutter" T-Bone. The fight is short and Zoro joins the crew again in the Rocket Man.

E262.note: After hiding under Robin's cloak for a few episodes, Sogeking finally shows himself. They stumble into the room with the CP9 and meet with Sanji and Franky.

E263.note: Robin willingly follows Blueno into his improvised door and Sogeking and Sanji are left alone. For a while, Blueno explains a little of Robin's past involving the Buster Call.

E264.note: As the Straw Hats and their allies are about to reach Enies Lobby, Paulie sketches the island's terrain and explains that, to save Robin, the crew must reach her.

E265.note: Luffy easily gets past the second gate, while the Franky Family and the Galley-La foremen, following their initial plan, break the first gate's defenses.

E266.note: The Galley-La foremen and the leading members of the Franky Family arrive late at the second gate because they had to mount the Franky Family's king bulls (キングブル).

E267.note: Robin and Franky are brought before Spandam, while Luffy blasts through waves of marines. The Galley-La foremen and the Franky Family eventually slip by Oimo and open the second gate.

E268.note: The Straw Hats struggle to catch up with Luffy, but get caught up in impeding each other's efforts; Sanji and Zoro indulge themselves in petty bickering.

E269.note: Spandam tells Robin that her crew has come to save her and that he, despite the agreement between her and the CP9, plans on taking them prisoner.

E270.note: Oimo explains to Usopp about how he and Kashii came to support the marines—the marines told them that they arrested their bosses: the giants Dorry.

E271.note: Kokoro and her granddaughter Chimeney contact the Straw Hats by means of Nami's portable transponder snail phone.

E272.note: Although Gomorrah was blinded, he continues on. Sanji and Zoro crush a building in the king bull's path and the party arrives at the courthouse.

E273.note: The Straw Hats storm the courthouse, where the remaining members of the Franky Family fight the three-headed judge Baskerville, and, aided by Yokozuna.

E274.note: Franky cannot understand why Robin refuses to be saved after Spandam broke his promise. While Luffy yells in the background, Franky decides to make Robin face him.

E275.note: Twenty years into the past, Robin is growing up on the island of Ohara. Her aunt, with whom she lives while her mother is away doing research, treats her poorly.

E276.note: Saul is shocked to learn that Robin is Nico Olvia's daughter and that he has been washed up on the shores of Ohara.

E277.note: Professor Clover is allowed to speak with one of the Elder Stars and reports of their research.

E278.note: Saul fights the battleships, attempting to allow Robin to escape. She tries to board the evacuation ship, but per Spandaine's orders, she is denied access.

E280.note: Zoro and Usopp's backstories are summarized.

E282.note: The backstories of Sanji and Chopper are summarized.

E283.note: The reasons for Nico Robin's recent actions as well as her backstory are summarized.

E284.note: Though initially intimidated when the Straw Hat Pirates declare war on the World Government, Spandam regains his composure.

E285.note: After emerging from the rubble caused by Rocket Man crashing into the Tower of Law, the Straw Hats and Franky are greeted by the blabbermouth CP9 agent Fukurou.

E286.note: As Luffy decides to make his way to the Gates of Justice to catch up to Robin and her captors, Nami and Sanji have their respective encounters with Kumadori and Kalifa.

E287.note: Finding themselves handcuffed after Kaku's man beast enhanced Tempest Kick damaged the Tower of Law, Zoro and Usopp learn that each of the keys are numbered.

E288.note: As Nami reaches Kalifa while Chopper holds Kumadori at bay, Luffy is saved from drowning by Chimeney and Gonbe.

E289.note: Refreshed after having placed three full bottles of cola in his stomach compartment, Franky turns the tide in his battle with Fukurou as they take their battle outside.

E290.note: As Zoro's strategy to use Usopp as a weapon falls apart, Chopper's breather concludes as an overweight Kumadori frees himself from the fridge after eating everything inside.

E291.note: In the Edo period-based alternate reality Grand Jipang, the small girl Rika takes an ill person, whom everyone calls Tot-san (which means "father" in Japanese).

E292.note: It is the seventh day of the new year in Grand Jipang.

E293.note: Having taken three Rumble Balls, Chopper transforms into his berserk Monster Point form and easily defeats Kumadori despite his attempts to subdue the instinct-driven beast.

E294.note: At the Courthouse Plaza, the marines defeat Oimo and Kashii and capture the Straw Hats' allies in the courthouse.

E295.note: Lucci and Luffy begin their mutually anticipated duel while Kalifa manages to subject Nami to her Golden Time attack to completely smooth her into a weakling.

E296.note: The fight between Nami and Kalifa climaxes when the former unveils her Fata Morgana ability and the latter makes a final attempt to take Nami and her illusion clones out.

E297.note: After pulling the unconscious Chopper out of the sea once he returned to his usual form, making his peace with the reindeer.

E298.note: As Sanji takes over fighting Jabra, Nami drags Usopp from the fight to go after Robin. But while Nami intended to go down stairs, Usopp has a better idea.

E299.note: Spandam drags Robin along the Bridge of Hesitation while down stairs in the room where Luffy is fighting Lucci, while Franky arrives and offers his help.

E300.note: After being relentlessly attacked, while hearing Kaku insulting Robin, Zoro's resulting rage manifested itself in a dark aura that Kaku thought to be a demon.

E301.note: After the Gate of Law has completely opened, having managed to loosened Robin's tooth grip on the bridge's rim, Spandam ties a rope around her waist.

E302.note: Unable to go back across the Bridge of Hesitation as one of the naval ships blasts the top floors of the Tower of Law into the waterfall, Robin.

E303.note: It is springtime in Grand Jipang. One day remains until the yearly town festival, in which everyone gathers to have a picnic under the 1000-year-old sakura tree.

E304.note: The Buster Call ships have reached Enies Lobby, passing to Bridge of Hesitation to spare Robin as she is traumatized by the onslaught she is forced to witness a second time in her life.

E305.note: After Rob Lucci is knocked into his ship by Luffy's enlarged fist, Vice Admiral Doberman orders his men to stand down when they failed to recognize the CP9 Agent.

E306.note: After fighting Lucci in Gear Third for a minute, barely escaping cannon fire in time, Luffy is left to cope.

E307.note: Franky and Robin finished throwing the last remaining marines off their commandeered convoy ship when Kokoro, in mermaid form, jumps aboard with the rest of the Straw Hats, Chimeney.

E308.note: The fight between Luffy and Lucci reaches its climax as the CP9 assassin makes use of his most powerful technique: the Six Kings Gun.

E309.note: While the Straw Hats struggle to hold their own against those among Buster Call's fighting elite who have Devil Fruit powers.

E310.note: As everyone is in awe of Luffy defeating Rob Lucci, it revealed by Transponder Snail loudspeakers that the Franky Family and Galley-La foremen faked their deaths.

E311.note: Despite appearing to have sunk at Water 7, the Going Merry somehow made it to Enies Lobby with the Straw Hats have little time to speculate how their ship arrived as a livid Spandam.

E312.note: With Enies Lobby and the Buster Call fleet out of sight, Usopp having resumed his Sniper King guise while everyone was still puzzled over how the Going Merry reach them.

E313.note: Two days later after returning to Water 7, Franky learns of Iceberg's plan to make the city into a ship in response of Aqua Laguna's intensity.

E314.note: While Garp is revealed to be Luffy's grandfather, his harsh upbringing methods explaining the pirate's endurance while criticizing his grandson's choice to become a pirate.

E315.note: After fixing the damage he caused, advised by his men to claim that the Straw Hats eluded him for the record, Garp returns to his ship while allowing Coby.

E316.note: A fleet of navy ships could not keep "Red-Haired" Shanks from meeting with Whitebeard, the World Government on high alert over two pirate Emperors crossing paths.

E317.note: While searching for supplies, Luffy and Chopper are approached by a girl named Abi to search for her lost yagara bull Bluefin, who had appeared to her in a dream.

E318.note: As the others look for supplies, Zoro strolls the city in search of a replacement katana despite lacking money to afford a new sword.

E319.note: While Chimeney and Gonbe accompany him to gather groceries, Sanji is forced by the two to take them to a small floating restaurant owned by a drunken old man for lunch.

E320.note: While the Gallery-La foremen take a break after the Franky Family provide a meal for the workers, Franky.

E321.note: When the Straw Hats arrive at the coast, Iceburg shows them the ship while Franky is nowhere to be seen.

E322.note: Despite Luffy offering to return his swimsuit if he agrees to join his crew, as the town populace convince to denounce his nudity.

E323.note: With Franky now a member of the Straw Hats, the crew set sail to evade Garp as he returned to Water 7 with direct orders from Fleet-Admiral Sengoku.

E324.note: With the crew back together, the Straw Hats can intensify their efforts to escape while agreeing to use the name that Iceberg, thinking Franky's figurehead is a sun rather than a lion.

E325.note: Three days earlier before the Thousand Sunny's maiden voyage, on an island near Water 7, Blackbeard receives the news of the Straw Hats' devastation of Enies Lobby.

R4.note: One Piece: Episode of Merry - The Tale of One More Friend revisits earlier story material and is placed around Post-Enies Lobby.
