E327.note: Though the fishermen attempt to retreat, the Straw Hats' refused to let them leave when they assumed they kidnapped Chopper.

E328.note: Trapped by moving icebergs, the Straw Hats try to figure a way out while avoiding being crushed.

E329.note: Using the Shark Submarine, Nami and Franky find out that penguins are moving the icebergs before they are attacked with Usopp and Sanji knocked off the Thousand Sunny.

E330.note: Brindo reveals his family possesses the Straw Hat flag, provoking Luffy to follow him to Lovely Land. But Puzzle captures Brindo and binds him with his chains to the mast.

E331.note: Due to Sanji's ideals, he and Usopp ended up captured by Albelle and Salchow.

E332.note: When Zoro attempts to reclaim the Straw Hats' flag, he provoked Don Accino as he reveals the power of the Heat Heat Fruit: the ability to melt anything within his aura of heat.

E333.note: Seeing that Luffy had arrived on Lovely Island, and Zoro running off to find Don Accino's chambers, Nami, Sanji, Usopp and Franky decide to distract Luffy while Robin finds their flag.

E334.note: The Heat Heat Fruit gives Accino all sorts of powers, from throwing blasts of heat, heating his skin to extreme temperatures, to even being able to fly by blowing steam out his nose.

E335.note: After a grueling struggle, Luffy finally defeats Don Accino with a red-hot Bazooka.

E336.note: Chopper Man and Namifia are low on money. Dr.

E337.note: In search of food, the Straw Hats attempt octopus hunting before discovering a barrel in the middle of the ocean.

E338.note: On board the Thousand Sunny, with everyone save Luffy unnerved by his appearance as an undead creature, Brook reveals that he is actually a human with Devil Fruit powers.

E339.note: Brook bids farewell to the Straw Hats and leaps overboard, revealing that the lightness of his body allows him to dash across the ocean's surface.

E340.note: Usopp, Nami and Chopper traverse through the forest of Thriller Bark, Chopper revealing Doctor Hogback to be a famous surgeon and expressing excitement to see him.

E341.note: After the meeting with Hogback, who frightens Chopper by insisting that no one is allowed in his laboratory, Nami takes a shower with Usopp and Chopper outside.

E342.note: While evading the Surprise Zombies upon finding a secret passage, Nami, Chopper and Usopp discover the truth about Victoria Cindry being a starlet who died long ago.

E343.note: After beating the zombies, planting them into the ground upon learning they ambushed Nami, Usopp and Chopper.

E344.note: Usopp, Nami and Chopper are effortlessly incapacitated by Ryuma and locked inside a coffin.

E345.note: After awakening the General Zombies, Absalom is accosted by Lola, a zombie warhog obsessed with making him her husband.

E346.note: As Perona makes her way to the Thousand Sunny to get the Straw Hats' treasure while Absalom attempts to stop Lola from hunting Nami, who realizes Innupe's uncanniness to Sanji, Luffy.

E347.note: Despite Absalom's attempts to halt Lola and abduct Nami to be her bride, both intents failed as the latter flees from Lola with Usopp and Chopper unable to slow the warthog zombie down.

E348.note: When Usopp and Chopper are unable to halt Lola, Nami manages to quell the warthog's bloodlust by claiming to be a male crossdresser.

E349.note: After defeating Taralan and releasing his shadow soul from within, Brook explains to Robin and Franky the truth that the zombies of Thriller Bark are actually corpses animated.

E350.note: With Luffy's shadow, Moria and his followers head into the freezer holding his ultimate special zombie that he believes will help him gain power in the New World.

E351.note: With Usopp, Chopper and Nami bearing witness, Moria implants Luffy's shadow inside the corpse of Oars.

E352.note: Brook confronts Ryuma, recalling his time on Thriller Bark five years prior - and his first battle with Ryuma and how he lost to a zombie with his own shadow.

E353.note: Usopp, Chopper, Robin and Franky return to the Sunny, discovering their ship had been ransacked before waking up Luffy, Zoro and Sanji.

E354.note: Luffy, Usopp, Sanji, and Zoro tell the rest of the crew how they got to meet Laboon as they started their new journey.

E355.note: After Luffy made up his mind to recruit Brook's story with the others supporting him, the Straw Hats decide to mount an assault on Thriller Bark to reclaim their stolen shadows.

E356.note: Luffy continues on to take on Moria while Chopper and Robin remain behind to take on Hogback who sics Jigoro and Inuppe on them.

E357.note: As Usopp continues to stand up to Perona's Negative Hollows while wiping out Wild Zombies, Brook is greatly overpowered by Ryuma was about to defeat the skeleton when Zoro arrives.

E358.note: As Luffy finds himself facing Gecko Moria's animated shadow Doppelman while attempting to attack Moria, Usopp manages to dispatch the Wild Zombies and chases.

E359.note: Beating Absalom when he refused to reveal he has been using firearms.

E360.note: Usopp is forced to go through a gauntlet of Perona's Horo-Horo powers, including an untouchable body, size manipulation, passing right through him.

E361.note: Having deduced that Perona used her Horo-Horo powers to project her spirit, Usopp attempts to fire an attack at it.

E362.note: Five minutes before the fight ends on the rooftop, Zoro duels Ryuma in Hogback's lab while Franky guards over the injured Brook.

E363.note: Chopper and Robin are outmatched by Jigoro and Inuppe, whose personalities lost most semblance to Zoro and Sanji.

E364.note: With Oars now a fully obedient zombie, Gecko Moria takes his leave while instructing the giant to hunt down the Straw Hat pirates whose wanted posters are sewn on his arm.

E365.note: As Abalsom makes another attempt to finalize his marriage to Nami, the other Straw Hats are fretting over Oars hunting them all down.

E366.note: Absalom, still battered from his fight with Sanji, attempts to once more seal his marriage to Nami with a kiss before she regains conscious.

E367.note: After being updated by Lola, Nami decides to find her crew after helping herself to Thriller Bark's treasure.

E368.note: Taking advantage of several weaknesses, including his stupidity and similarities to Luffy, the Straw Hats continue to wear away at Oars despite his continued persistence.

E369.note: Despite Usopp's attempts to reason him about fighting Oars as Luffy is fighting Gecko Moria, Zoro explains that their captain has most likely been tricked by now.

E370.note: With Moria's guidance, Oars is better able to counter the Straw Hats' strategies and turn their attacks against them.

E371.note: Moria explains that he is using his ability Shadow Revolution to manipulate Oar's body by distorting the giant's shadow.

E372.note: With Zoro risking his own life by distracting Oars before the giant knees him into a wall, Usopp shoots Brook's salt into Oars' mouth with Kuwagata.

E373.note: Though Oars recovers from Nightmare Luffy's attack, despite their initial reaction to flee, the Rolling Pirates find Luffy joined by his revived crew as they act out a final attack.

E374.note: With Zoro telling the frighten Rolling Pirates that Gecko Moria's Shadows Asgard is nothing but an act of desperation to stall for sunrise at the cost of his mind.

E375.note: Despite nearly being vaporized, Luffy, Zoro, Sanji, Robin, and the Rolling Pirates regain their shadows at the last second along with the rest of Moria's victims.

E376.note: Zoro attacks Kuma, who reveals that he has eaten the Paw Paw fruit, and shows his ability to repel attacks, move considerable distances almost instantly.

E377.note: With almost everyone on the island is knocked out in the blast, Kuma was about to grab Luffy when he was attacked by Zoro.

E378.note: Hogback and Absalom escape Thriller Bark with the unconscious Moria, revealing that Blackbeard's Warlord status from defeating Ace.

E379.note: Brook recalls his past, when the whale Laboon started following his pirate ship and befriended the crew.

E380.note: Brook recalls the Rumbar Pirates' journey through the Grand Line, where they faced many dangers but still managed to enjoy themselves.

E381.note: Brook tells Luffy that he is glad to be alive and asks if he can join the crew, which Luffy approves of.

E382.note: The Straw Hats take a vacation at Spa Island, a ship with luxury facilities that even serves pirates and criminals, but while there, they encounter Foxy and his crew.

E383.note: After Lina reveals that she has searched all over the ship, Luffy decides to destroy the ship in order to find Sayo.

E384.note: Brook, having heard of the Straw Hats' adventures while reading their logbook, and not wanting to become a burden to them in the New World, decides to help out around the ship.

E385.note: Sengoku complains about Kuma's sparing the Straw Hats, but Garp is confident that they will not talk about defeating Moria.

E386.note: Camie and Pappagu introduce themselves to the Straw Hats, who then later find out that their fish-man friend has been kidnapped by Iron Mask Duval and the Macro Gang.

E387.note: The Straw Hats arrive at Duval's base noticing how quiet it is. They then find Camie's fish-man friend in a cage, only to recognize him as Hatchan, a former member of Arlong's crew.

E388.note: The Flying Fish Riders launch their attack on the Straw Hats. Luffy tries riding one of them, but ends up underwater.

E389.note: Duval reveals that he was once a small-time bandit until Sanji's wanted poster came to light. Since then, he hid his face with the iron mask until he would get revenge on Sanji.

E390.note: With the Flying Fish Riders taken care of, the Straw Hats rest as Hatchan treats them to the promised Takoyaki.

E391.note: After landing on the Sabaody Archipelago, the Straw Hats begin searching for a man Hatchan knows who can coat their ship so they can continue on to Fish-man Island.

E392.note: Luffy and his group defeat some bounty hunters who were attempting to capture them.

E393.note: Luffy's group begins to look for Rayleigh and "searches" the islands local amusement park.

E394.note: Duval and his crew arrive on the archipelago and start gathering the Straw Hats up to bring them to the Human Auction House, where the kidnappers have brought Camie.

E395.note: The human auction finally begins, and it is only then that the Straw Hats discovered that their mermaid friend is being sold off at Grove 1.

E396.note: The auction continues, but Saint Charloss arrives and ruins the Straw Hats' plan to buy Camie back by placing an exorbitant bid on her.

E397.note: After Luffy punches Charloss, the guards begin to attack the Straw Hats, and most of the observers, except for Charloss' family and Kid and Law's crews, evacuate.

E398.note: News of the attack on the World Nobles reaches the pirates in the town, as well as the Navy Headquarters, where Kizaru decides to go to the island in order to deal with the situation.

E399.note: Luffy, Law and Kid's crews join the fight, defeat the rest of the Navy outside the auction house, promising to meet and fight in the New World.

E400.note: Upon arriving back at Shakky's Bar, Rayleigh reveals to the Straw Hats that he was first mate to the legendary Gold Roger and the truth to his crew's dismissal.

E401.note: Admiral Kizaru appears in the Archipelago and demonstrates his mysterious light abilities before taking on the Supernova Basil Hawkins, while Urouge and Drake fight a Pacifista.

E402.note: The Straw Hat Pirates fight against one of the Pacifistas, and while it does not have Kuma's Devil Fruit powers, it is still able to withstand their strongest attacks.

E403.note: The Straw Hats combine their strongest attacks to defeat the Pacifista, but are exhausted after the battle.

E404.note: Usopp and Brook's attempts to attack Kizaru fail, but Rayleigh arrives, managing to block Kizaru's attack and hold him off.

E405.note: Sentomaru reveals that Kuma has the ability to make people fly through the sky to far away places.

E406.note: In an alternate universe set in Edo period Japan, the Mikoshi race is about to take place, the winner of which receives a million Berries, and Boss Luffy.

E407.note: Robin reveals Brook's past to Luffy and his friends, and they decide to help him. Brook is captured by Thriller LTD, but Luffy and Zoro arrive to help him escape.

E408.note: Three days have passed since the incident at Sabaody Archipelago. Having been hit by Kuma, Luffy is teleported to an unknown island.

E409.note: Luffy realizes that the island's name is Amazon Lily, an island populated entirely by the Kuja tribe of women warriors that men are not permitted to set foot on.

E410.note: Luffy resolves to leave the island, reunite with his crew and defeat the enemies who had overpowered them earlier, and decides to borrow a ship.

E411.note: Hancock's ship reaches Amazon Lily and she returns to her palace.

E412.note: Luffy looks at Hancock's back and notes that he saw what was on it before.

E413.note: Luffy gets the petrified Margaret and her friends to safety and then fights against Sandersonia and Marigold, but struggles against their Devil Fruit powers.

E414.note: Luffy activates Gear Second and gains the upper hand.

E415.note: Sandersonia and Marigold concede defeat to Luffy, and Hancock agrees to grant Luffy the choice of getting a ship or saving Margaret, and he chooses the latter.

E416.note: Following a party, Luffy learns from Nyon that Hancock is a Warlord of the Sea, and that they have been called together as the Navy is launching an all-out offensive on Whitebeard.

E417.note: Nyon discovers that Hancock's disease is the same one that killed her two predecessors, and has Hancock meet with Luffy.

E418.note: Luffy proceeds to Impel Down, but worries about his friends.

E419.note: Chopper lands on an island known as Birdie Kingdom, and finds himself in the nest of two large birds who play with him before accidentally dropping him into the forest below.

E420.note: Robin finds herself on Tequila Wolf, a snow-covered island.

E421.note: Zoro lands on the island where Kuma sent Perona. Perona treats his wounds, but forces him to stay while subjecting him to humiliating treatment.

E422.note: Garp tells Ace that Luffy now knows about his parentage and that he had hoped Ace and Luffy would become marines.

E423.note: Hancock petrifies Domino and the camera snail to allow Luffy to sneak out before submitting to the search.

E424.note: Luffy easily defeats the Blugori while Buggy is unable to defeat even one.

E425.note: Luffy and Buggy find themselves in the Floor of Wild Beasts from Hell and fight the monsters there.

E426.note: In a flashback, it is revealed that 20 years ago, a pirate named "Gold Lion" Shiki cut off his legs and escaped from Impel Down.

E427.note: The Straw Hats meet with people in places that remind them of where they came from, and Yoko becomes irritated at how easily they are bonding.

E428.note: The rest of the Straw Hats hear the fighting on the island and decide to head over.

E429.note: After escaping from the net, Luffy knocks Largo far away and goes off to fight him.

OVA1.note: One Piece Film: Strong World Episode 0 is an OVA placed around Little East Blue. Strong World prequel; watch before Film: Strong World.

M10.note: One Piece Film: Strong World is a movie placed around Little East Blue. Watch after episodes 426-429 (Little East Blue tie-in).

E430.note: Ace speaks with Jimbei, a fishman who is one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea, in his cell, and learns that Jimbei had wanted to stop the war between Whitebeard's pirates.

E431.note: With the Sphinx having fallen to the next level, a riot breaks out on Level 2 as the prisoners leave their cells.

E432.note: Buggy and Mr. 3 come across Mr. 2's cell, and release him, telling him Luffy is in the prison, working his way towards level 5.

E433.note: Luffy and Mr. 2 fight their way through Level 3, eventually encountering the Minotaurus, and Luffy defeats it with Jet Bazooka.

E434.note: After the Minotaurus' defeat, the floor gives way from the attacks Luffy and his companions used to defeat it, and they fall into Level 4.

E435.note: Luffy begins fighting Magellan despite Bon Clay's warnings to flee, and finds that he is unable to go on the offensive because of Magellan's poisonous body.

E436.note: Hannyabal quickly defeats Buggy and Mr. 3. Luffy becomes weaker as a result of absorbing Magellan's poison, and is no longer able to fight effectively.

E437.note: After Luffy's defeat, Magellan has him imprisoned in Level 5 until the poison kills him. Mr.

E438.note: Mr. 2 apologizes for abandoning Luffy and searches for Ivankov.

E439.note: Emporio Ivankov displays some of his powers, including stopping a vengeful pirate's cannon shot merely by winking and then turning him into a woman.

E440.note: Ivankov tells Bon Clay that he has eaten the Horm-Horm Fruit, which enables him to manipulate people's hormones for various purposes, and that New Kama Land lies between Level 5.

E441.note: After Luffy emerges from the treatment, Ivankov learns that he is Dragon's son and reveals himself as one of Dragon's commanders in the revolutionary army.

E442.note: Luffy, Ivankov and Inazuma reach Level 6 after Ace has been taken away, and are nearly caught in a gas trap until Inazuma uses his Snip Snip powers in order to cut up the floor.

E443.note: Luffy is reluctant to free Crocodile, but Ivankov offers to keep him in line by blackmailing him with a weakness he knows about.

E444.note: While Luffy and his allies continue fighting on Level 4, Buggy and Mr. 3 reach Level 2 and release the prisoners there to cause a riot to cover their escape.

E445.note: The Demon Guards arrive and overpower many of the prisoners, but Luffy, Jimbei and Crocodile defeat them in a single attack each. Buggy and Mr.

E446.note: Hannyabal fights against Luffy, but is outmatched against his Gear Second ability.

E447.note: After Luffy punches Blackbeard, Blackbeard retaliates by slamming him into the ground, injuring him by nullifying his rubber powers.

E448.note: As Luffy and the escaping prisoners pass through Level 3, Magellan catches up with them. Ivankov and Inazuma stay behind to stall him, but both of them are defeated.

E449.note: Luffy becomes enraged after learning about Magellan defeating Ivankov and Inazuma, but Mr. 2 and Jimbei persuade him that escaping takes priority.

E450.note: Magellan uses a move known as "Venom Demon" that corrodes anything it touches, rendering even Mr. 3's wax ineffective and forcing Luffy and Mr. 3 to retreat. Mr.

E451.note: Luffy and the rest of the prisoners make it onto the ship with the help of the whale sharks, but the Gates of Justice are still closed and the ship is under attack.

E452.note: Shiryu saves Blackbeard and his crew by giving them antidotes to Magellan's poison before taking the opportunity to join with them.

E453.note: As Luffy's ship heads to Marine Headquarters, the scene shifts to a point some time in the past.

E454.note: Sanji desperately tries to escape the crossdressers of Kamabakka Kingdom, until he eventually encounters Caroline, the "queen" stand-in for Ivankov.

E455.note: Robin is imprisoned in a tower on Tequila Wolf and interrogated for information on the whereabouts of the Straw Hats, but her captors keep her true identity a secret.

E456.note: Zoro tries to recover his swords, but fails to understand Perona's simple directions, thwarting her plan to get his hopes up and use Negative Hollow on him.

E457.note: Luffy recalls his childhood with Ace, meeting him again in Alabasta and receiving his Vivre card, meeting Blackbeard without knowing who he was, hearing the news about Ace's execution.

E458.note: Around three hours before the execution, the five Warlords present for the battle and three Marine Admirals move to their positions to await Whitebeard's attack.

E459.note: Ace reaches the scaffold in Marineford, where the Marines assemble for Whitebeard's assault.

E460.note: While the Marines are waiting for Whitebeard's assault and Ace's execution, Garp has a flashback: months before Gold Roger was executed.

E461.note: Whitebeard reveals his Devil Fruit powers in front of the Marines. Whitebeard and Ace's past are shown: Ace was the captain of the Spade Pirates.

E462.note: Whitebeard and his allies declare to the Marines and the Warlords their determination to rescue Ace, while Luffy and his group continue to reach Marineford.

E463.note: Jozu manages to withstand Mihawk's slash with his Devil Fruit power, which turns his body into diamond.

E464.note: Oars Jr. forces his way into the plaza, overturning one of the Marine ships and defeating the Giant Unit, enabling the Whitebeard Pirates to advance.

E465.note: Oars Jr. tries to reach towards Ace, but is unable to and falls down. Vice Admiral Ronse launched a sneak attack at Whitebeard, but was easily defeated by a quake punch.

E466.note: In a flashback, the tsunami Whitebeard created rushes toward Luffy's Marine ship. They attempt to ride the wave, but become stuck on the top when Aokiji freezes it.

E467.note: Luffy charges forward to save Ace, fighting off the marines in the way.

E468.note: Ace is still upset that the Whitebeard Pirates and Luffy are still trying to save him, but decides to accept whether he lives or dies.

E469.note: Doflamingo tells Ivankov that Kuma had slowly been modified into a Pacifista over time, and a few days ago, had his brain replaced, completely losing his personality.

E470.note: Mihawk begins attacking Luffy while Hancock and Ivankov are occupied, easily defeating two of the New Kamas who arrive to assist him and knocking Jimbei aside.

E471.note: The Pacifistas approach from behind, defeating the Whitebeard Pirates in their path, but the plan is not fully effective because of Whitebeard attacking the ships at the sides.

E472.note: Squard's stabbing of Whitebeard shocks the Whitebeard pirates, as does his statement that Whitebeard agreed to sacrifice his allies to save Ace.

E473.note: In a desperate attempt to stop Whitebeard from advancing to the execution platform, the Marines activate walls to block Whitebeard's path, but is stopped by the ice.

E474.note: Akainu's Meteor Volcano melts the nearly all the ice and destroys the Whitebeard Pirates' ships, dropping them into boiling water.

E475.note: Luffy attempts to slip past the admirals, but Kizaru easily catches up to him and knock him to the ground.

E476.note: Whitebeard attacks the Marines in the plaza, and Aokiji comes to fight him until third division Leader Jozu arrives to handle him.

E477.note: Ace thinks back to his childhood, when he fought with anyone who spoke ill of Roger, and wondering if he should ever have been born.

E478.note: Ivankov warns Luffy that the hormones are only fooling his body, and he will not be able to get up if he falls again, before the two advance, fighting the Marines in their way.

E479.note: After Luffy unleashes his Haki, the Marines move to eliminate him, seeing the potential threat he poses, and Whitebeard orders his men to back him up.

E480.note: Garp faces off with Luffy and thinks back to when he trained him so that he could become a Marine and stay on the right path.

E481.note: Ace and Luffy fight off the Marines as the Whitebeard pirates move to escape.

E482.note: Whitebeard's final order leaves his pirates distraught as they leave Marineford for the New World.

E483.note: Akainu's attack against Ace gives him only seconds to live. Akainu tries to attack Ace again, but Jimbei blocks it, and Marco, freed from his handcuffs by Mr.

E484.note: After witnessing the death of Ace at the hands of Akainu, Luffy becomes completely incapacitated.

E485.note: Blackbeard reveals that the entire reason he wanted to become one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea was to gain access to Impel Down, and that he gained entrance.

E486.note: News of Whitebeards's death spreads across the world, and many people are excited to hear of the demise of one of the world's most powerful pirates.

E487.note: Blackbeard expresses his desire to sink Marineford into the ocean after destroying the fortress, but Sengoku attacks him, saying that the island is a symbol of justice in the world.

E488.note: The fighting between the pirates and the Marines continues as Sengoku and Garp fight Blackbeard, and the Marines are determined to eliminate the fleeing Whitebeard pirates.

E489.note: Shanks tricks Buggy into returning Luffy's straw hat to him, noting that while he would like to see Luffy again, it is too soon for their reunion.

E490.note: The public celebrates the end of the war around the world. Meanwhile, in Marineford, Sengoku is being told that Blackbeard released the Impel Down inmates to join his crew earlier.

E491.note: As a result of Whitebeard's death, pirates over the world attack islands while heading to the Grand Line, while islands formally under Whitebeard's protection fall under siege.

E492.note: Toriko and the Straw Hats follow the assailant's tracks and set out to rescue their comrades, Nami and Komatsu.

E493.note: A flashback of Luffy's life is shown. Ten years before Luffy's journey, Garp takes his grandson to Mt. Corvo.

E494.note: Luffy discovers the junkyard is named the Gray Terminal. Luffy runs after Ace when he steals the treasure and run into Sabo, a boy who lives there.

E496.note: Luffy's attempt at using the Gum-Gum Pistol technique backfires. Luffy is no match for Ace and Sabo, and is defeated.

E497.note: Luffy, Sabo, and Ace leave the Dadan Family mainly because they need to hide from Garp, who disapprove of their dreams of becoming pirates and also because of Bluejam.

E498.note: Ace, Sabo, and Luffy chase down a Danpa and succeeds to catch it, but a tiger steals it.

E499.note: While Ace and Sabo are chased by the tiger, they encounter Naguri again. The brothers build a ship for Naguri during their training.

E500.note: While gathering supplies in the Grey Terminal, Sabo, Luffy and Ace are captured by Bluejam's crew.

E501.note: In High Town, Sabo fails to warn his brothers as he is caught by his father. Meanwhile, Bluejam tells Ace and Luffy about the fire at the Gray Terminal.

E502.note: Dadan knocks Bluejam's shot astray and confronts him. Dadan gives Bluejam an ultimatum: he will let Ace go, or Dadan will defeat him.

E503.note: Saint Jalmack shoots at Sabo's boat again, destroying it and killing the sole occupant. Ace and Dadan return to her house much to the relief of Luffy and Dadan's bandits.

E504.note: After the death of Sabo, Ace and Luffy continue their training while the others discover the Revolutionaries' ship.

E505.note: At Windmill Village, Garp visits Dadan and the mountain bandits. Dadan fights the Vice Admiral. Makino stops Dadan and tells her that Garp is suffering as a result of Ace's death.

E506.note: On Kuraigana Island, Zoro confronts and is nearly defeated several Humandrills, but is stopped by Mihawk.

E507.note: On Maiden Island, Rayleigh kills a Sea King and arrives on the island in front of Law's crew.

E508.note: At Amazon Lily, Luffy is trying to make up his mind regarding Rayleigh's offer, as he made a promise to his crew to meet in three days.

E509.note: On Kuraigana Island, Zoro tries to use Mihawk's boat to escape but is stopped by the humandrills. He also discovers that the humandrills have used Zoro's techniques.

E510.note: Jimbei and Luffy hijack a Marine ship and enter Marineford again using the Gates of Justice. At the Birdie Kingdom, the local tribe gives Chopper a basket of fruit and cotton candy.

E511.note: Luffy finally arrives at Marineford along with Rayleigh and Jimbei.

E512.note: Buggy tearfully reunites with his crew on an unknown island. Alvida discovers a treasure mark from which Luffy found in Impel Down. Mr.

E513.note: Near the Sabaody Archipelago, Law and his crew decide to wait for the proper moment to enter the New World. On a winter island, X.

E514.note: On Weatheria, Nami is cornered by the scientists again.

E515.note: At the Tehna Gehna Kingdom, Brook is displayed to the townsfolk while reading the newspaper. Brook tries to train himself so he can reach 40 degrees, but this fails.

E516.note: Jimbei thanks Luffy for what he has done to him. Vowing to meet again at Fishman Island, Jimbei and Luffy go their separate ways.

M11.note: One Piece 3D: Straw Hat Chase is a movie placed around Post-War. Watch after episode 516 / before the timeskip return.

SP8.note: One Piece 3D2Y: Overcoming Ace's Death! Luffy's Pledge to His Friends is a TV special placed around Post-War. Set during the timeskip; watch after episode 516.

E517.note: Two years have passed since the war at Marineford, and the Straw Hat Pirates have since trained for their return.

E518.note: Using a fake disguise provided by Hancock, Luffy and the Kuja tribe go their separate ways and he confronts the Fake Straw Hats.

E519.note: Robin finally makes it to the Thousand Sunny and reunites with Franky. The archaeologist reveals to Franky that she is the eighth person who saw Rayleigh, as Luffy has yet to appear.

E520.note: At the Sabao Dome, Brook finishes his song. Meanwhile, Caribou frightens a Marine, digging a live grave with Coribou, and Fake Luffy rallies the pirates.

E521.note: After Brook escapes from the Marines at the Sabao Dome with a Rosy Life Rider, a riot is going on in the dome. Meanwhile, Luffy is caught before the Fake Straw Hats.

E522.note: After Luffy and Rayleigh go their separate ways, Rayleigh draws a line to prevent the Marines from coming any further to the Straw Hats.

SP14.note: One Piece Fan Letter is a TV special placed around Return to Sabaody. Set around Return to Sabaody; story-order placement after episode 522.

E523.note: At the Sabaody Archipelago, Caribou has his men bury the Fake Straw Hats and departs to find the real Straw Hats.

E524.note: After ramming his ship into the Thousand Sunny, Caribou orders his men to attack, but Mohmoo, frightened by Luffy's and Sanji's appearance, flees with Caribou's crew.

E525.note: Sanji uses his Blue Walk technique to more underwater at the speed of a fishman before burning alone of the Kraken's tentacles with a Diable Jambe while Zoro cuts off another.

E526.note: The captain of the Flying Dutchman reveals himself as Vander Decken IX. He orders Wadatsumi and Ankoro to kill the Straw Hats.

E527.note: One of the fishmen surrounding the Straw Hats says that his name is Hammond and he is a member of the New Fishman Pirates. He offers Luffy to join them, but is turned down.

E528.note: Nami has survived by landing on another part of Fishman Island. Using his Coup De Vent technique, Franky rescues Robin and regroups with Nami.

E529.note: Sanji is given a transfusion from two okamas on the Mermaid Cafe, a local restaurant in the port town of Coral Hill.

E530.note: In the open sea, Gyro remembers the time when he was attacked by the New Fishman Pirates and vowing never to return to Fishman Island.

E531.note: On Fishman Island, Caribou breaks out of the barrel and traps Ishilly and the mermaids with his Swamp-Swamp Fruit powers.

E532.note: On the sea floor, Vander Decken IX reveals that he plans to marry the princess, using his Mark-Mark Fruit curse to throw a giant axe at her.

E533.note: At the Ryugu Palace, Nami, Usopp, Brook and Zoro tie up King Neptune, the Ministers and the palace guards. They have an argument over whether their next plan should be.

E534.note: On the southeastern part of the Fishman Island, Franky and Tom's brother Den discuss about the Thousand Sunny and discovers that ships are swept away into the tides.

E535.note: On Coral Hill, Hordy remarks that this will be the last day of the Ryugu Kingdom as the fishmen are here to drive the humans out of the island.

E536.note: At the Ryugu Palace, Hordy says that he will destroy Neptune's soldiers and realize Arlong's ambitions once again. He proceeds to destroy the palace by flooding it.

E537.note: The citizens of Fishman Island are left in a panic that Shirahoshi has been kidnapped by Luffy. Despite Chopper's protests, Sanji finally turns around and is petrified.

E538.note: After Zoro renders Hordy unconscious, a fishman has his captain take an Energy Steroid and attack the group again.

E539.note: On Fishman Island, Ikaros orders the citizens to step on Otohime's portrait, Daruma chomps his way through the island's candy factory, Zeo.

E540.note: A flashback of Tiger and Jimbei's life is shown. Sixteen years before the Straw Hats enter Fishman Island, Queen Otohime confronts a thief. However, her hand is seriously injured.

E541.note: At Fishman Island, everyone celebrates Tiger's attack on Mariejois. Hordy, however, cheers them on for their goal to kill humans.

E542.note: Toriko and Luffy continue their search for the ingredients to save Chopper on To-Chuka Island. Note: This episode concludes a crossover which began in Episode 51 of Toriko.

E543.note: On the Sun Pirates' ship, Aladdin, the crew's doctor, allows Hatchan to let Koala do as she pleases, but Arlong tells her that he wants to kill the girl.

E544.note: Outraged after witnessing the death of Tiger, Arlong returns to Foulshoot Island to kill the humans, but Borsalino apprehends Arlong.

E545.note: The Sun Pirates go their separate ways after Jimbei becomes a Warlord. Otohime, however is determined that these actions would to help her cause.

E546.note: At the Ryugu Palace, Vander Decken proposes to marry her by using the Tamatebako in an attempt to make Shirahoshi age.

E547.note: Following Otohime's death, Hordy kills a human pirate and convinces the citizens that the human is responsible for killing the queen.

E548.note: Hordy announces that any fishman who is associated with humans will be exiled from the island and everyone is to migrate to the surface, leaving the people of Fishman Island in a panic.

E549.note: At the Ryugu Palace, Usopp, Zoro and Brook conceive a plan to escape. Brook reveals that he can remove his soul from his body.

E550.note: Hordy and his fishmen arrive at Conchcorde Plaza to execute Neptune.

E551.note: Neptune's army launches an offensive strike against Hordy, but they are completely wiped out and Hordy goes to Conchcorde Plaza.

E552.note: On their way to defeat Hordy, Jimbei, Shirahoshi and Megalo are captured by Hordy's forces. They are taken to Conchcorde Plaza just as the pirates tie up Neptune and the princes.

E553.note: Shirahoshi reveals that Megalo witnessed the assassination and told her about it, but chose to remain silent to honor her mother's last wish.

E554.note: The citizens marvel and celebrate at the arrival of the Straw Hats.

E555.note: The Straw Hat Pirates begin their attack with Luffy, Zoro and Sanji taking the lead in the fight but mostly it is the latter two of the three who begin showing their new powers.

E556.note: The battle rages on between the Straw Hats and the New Fishman Pirates. Sanji and Zoro are competing over who can take down the most fishmen.

E557.note: In Gyoncorde Plaza, the battle between the New Fishman Pirates and the Straw Hat Pirates continues, with the Straw Hats having gained the upper hand.

E558.note: King Neptune watches in shock over the sight of the giant ship Noah as Fukaboshi asks what is so important about a relic of the past.

E559.note: Shirahoshi begins swimming out to sea in an attempt to lure the ark Noah away and save Fishman Island, while Luffy gets Sanji's help to allow him to reach the ship's chains.

E560.note: Hordy takes his trident out of Decken's body and after a short fight, Decken is defeated.

E561.note: Luffy tries to protect Shirahoshi along with her three brothers helping also against Hordy Jones as the New Fishman Pirate Captain is hell bent on killing the Mermaid Princess.

E562.note: Vander Decken IX finally loses consciousness and the massive ship Noah starts to fall back slowly onto the fishman island.

E563.note: On Fishman Island, Jimbei and Sanji continue to fight Wadatsumi and Wadatsumi becomes angry and inflates himself unknowingly crushing some of his allies.

E564.note: As Fukaboshi begins to state what Hordy really is, Hordy's past is revealed as he recalls his hatred towards humans, the New Fishman Pirates officers are taught.

E565.note: As the fighting continues, Brook and Zeo continue their fight with Zeo landing an attack on Brook and decapitating him.

E566.note: Hordy uses to the Energy Steroids to recover and overpowers Luffy, biting him again in the same spot. Luffy retorts with another Red Hawk, but Hordy is still standing.

E567.note: After defeating the New Fishman Pirates, the Straw Hat Pirates are relaxing and waiting for Luffy.

E568.note: Following Hordy's defeat, Shirahoshi converses with the Sea Kings, who are surprised that Luffy is able to understand their conversation with Shirahoshi.

E569.note: As the whole island celebrates the capture and defeat of Hordy and his comrades by the Straw Hat Crew of whom make their own plans to leave Fishman Island soon.

E570.note: King Neptune talks about the Ancient Weapons and Shirahoshi being one of them to Robin while Caribou listens and makes way to Shirahoshi's room to capture and sell her.

E571.note: The rest of the Straw Hats are waiting for Luffy, Zoro, and Sanji to return with the treasure.

E572.note: During a check on the palace, the Minister of the Right gets a report that the Tamate Box is gone along with all the treasures and remembers something very important about it.

E573.note: The mermaids and Keimi from the Mermaid Cafe ask Sharley if they can say farewell to the Straw Hats.

E574.note: The Straw Hats are heading to the New World. Nami is taking a bath in the Thousand Sunny and Chopper comes in.

SP6.note: One Piece: Episode of Luffy - Adventure on Hand Island is a TV special placed around Fish-Man Island. Release-era placement after Fish-Man Island / before Film Z tie-ins.

E575.note: There is a whole fleet of Neo Marine ships, one of which houses Ain and Bins. The Straw Hats fight back against a fleet of Marine ships, but it turns out to be a mirage.

E576.note: Nami creates a plan for the Straw Hats to rescue Lily's father, Panz Fry.

E577.note: Several of Shuzo's men appear and fight against the Marines and easily begin to overwhelm them.

E578.note: Shuzo orders his men to be launched at the island like torpedoes and sacrifice themselves for him. He then attacks Panz Fry and Lily.

M12.note: One Piece Film: Z is a movie placed around Z's Ambition. Watch after episodes 575-578 (Z Ambition tie-in).

E579.note: The Straw Hats arrive at a volcanic island that does not appear on the New World Log Pose.

E580.note: Luffy and Zoro start fighting the dragon, which can speak and breathe fire. Luffy rockets up to the dragon's back, discovering a pair of legs.

E581.note: Luffy, Zoro, Usopp, and Robin stand near the dead dragon that Zoro killed, with Luffy trying to pull out the man who is stuck in the dragon's back.

E582.note: Franky, Sanji, Nami and Chopper are surprised that the samurai is still alive after being cut.

E583.note: The Straw Hats who escaped the prison cell end up in the Biscuit Room where they find a group of children. The samurai head scares the children.

E584.note: The G-5 marines attempt to reach the snow side of Punk Hazard and shoot the ice blocks with cannons to make a path.

E585.note: Chopper and Nami try to escape with the children until they come across a door that Chopper breaks open. They step inside a cold room with frozen people.

E586.note: Luffy, Zoro, Usopp and Robin try to get to the frozen side of Punk Hazard, but their ship gets capsized by Brownbeard.

E587.note: Feeling dishonored by still being alive, Tashigi tells Law to kill her but he says she doesn't have the right to tell him when or how she can die, and she tries to attack him.

E588.note: Tashigi tries to see what happened in the fight. Luffy's group sees the flying warship. Luffy sees Law and says thanks for saving him two years ago.

E589.note: The G-5 marines discuss that Caesar Clown is probably behind the Island and that the child abduction case in that area could be true.

E591.note: The children and Straw Hats are in a cave with Brownbeard tied up talking about the Master. Zoro, Sanji and Brook go to look for the samurai, who had gone to look for his torso.

E592.note: The Yeti Cool Brothers make their way to the destroyed lab where the Straw Hats and the children are.

E593.note: Chopper tells the others that Nami was kidnapped by the Yeti Cool Brothers.
