E803.note: Nami confronts Cracker and tries to use various tricks against him, but they prove ineffective and he overpowers her.

E804.note: Six months into his childhood imprisonment, Sanji remembers learning to cook and bringing food to his now-deceased mother against Judge's wishes.

E805.note: Nami produces rain to soften Cracker's Biscuit Soldiers, allowing Luffy to eat and disable them.

E806.note: Luffy is full from eating all of Cracker's biscuits for several hours, but refuses to give up, causing an enraged Cracker to attack him directly.

E807.note: Sanji has memories of his escape from the Germa Kingdom, meeting Zeff and later joining the Straw Hats as his family heads to the Whole Cake Chateau.

E808.note: Luffy refuses to back down from the hostile Sanji, causing Sanji to confront him and attack him with Diable Jambe.

E809.note: After hearing about Cracker's defeat, Big Mom creates a big storm before sending a massive army out to take revenge on Luffy.

E810.note: Luffy faces Big Mom's enraged army and decimates their hordes of Chess Soldiers.

E811.note: In the Mirro-World, Chopper and Carrot fight against Brûlée and her henchmen, but are subdued by their own reflections.

E812.note: Tamago increases security on the Big Mom Pirates' three Ponegliffs in the Room of Treasure by having Sweet Commander Charlotte Smoothie protect them.

E813.note: Big Mom informs Luffy that she found the Tamate Box among the treasure Luffy left her at Fishman Island and plans to open it at the wedding.

E814.note: Thanks to Pedro's distraction, Brook manages to sneak into the Room of Treasure. With the power of his Devil Fruit, he overwhelms the Chess Soldier homies by the power of soul.

E815.note: Pudding reveals a secret plan to prevent Sanji from marrying her, shocking Luffy and Nami. After she leaves, Luffy attempts to escape from their prison.

E816.note: Chopper and Carrot run through the Mirro-World with Brûlée as their hostage to find Luffy, Nami and Sanji.

E817.note: Luffy continues trying to escape the prison book by tearing off his hands.

E818.note: After revealing the truth about the wedding, Pudding removes Reiju's memories of their encounter with her Memo-Memo Fruit powers.

E819.note: Jimbei frees Luffy and Nami by burning the book they're trapped in, declaring an act of rebellion against Big Mom that he admits he cannot go back from.

E820.note: In the Chateau courtyard, Tamago regenerates into a stronger form with chicken features known as Viscount Hiyoko with his Egg-Egg Fruit power.

E821.note: As Pedro detonates a string of dynamite at the courtyard, Chopper grabs him into the Mirro-World, before Tamago, having regenerated into the rooster-like Count Niwatori.

E822.note: Having brought Jimbei and Nami into the Mirro-World, the Sanji Retrieval Team searches for Luffy, Sanji and Brook.

E823.note: After Sanji flees, Bobbin decides to pursue him by himself. The Sanji Retrieval Team finds the mirror leading to Brook but also learns that the mirror also leads to Big Mom's bedroom.

E824.note: Luffy fights against Charlotte Counter but despite his best efforts, his hunger causes him to be easily overpowered.

E825.note: After a harsh struggle, Luffy finally manages to defeat Counter. He then rests besides King Baum's corpse.

E826.note: After Luffy and Sanji reconcile, the Sanji Retrieval Team manages to contact them through a mirror shard.

E827.note: The Sanji Retrieval Team goes to the Fire Tank Pirates' hideout to meet Capone Bege.

E828.note: The meeting between the Sanji Retrieval Team and the Fire Tank Pirates begins.

E829.note: The meeting between the Sanji Retrieval Team and the Fire Tank Pirates continues. The groups review their plans for the Tea Party and intend to use a mirror and Brûlée to escape.

E830.note: The Underworld emperors arrive at the Whole Cake Chateau to attend Big Mom's tea party. Bege and his crew act as security for the wedding.

E831.note: As the Tea Party begins, the Fire Tank Pirates and Caesar Clown make preparations to commit their assassination attempt on Big Mom.

E832.note: The wedding begins and after Sanji and Pudding arrive at the venue, Streusen brings out the wedding cake with the altar at the top.

E833.note: Luffy's duplicates run amok at the wedding venue as the cake collapses. Following Bege's plan, Luffy goes after Carmel's photo.

E834.note: Even though Big Mom has a mental crisis, she does not start screaming as Bege had planned. Luffy and Jimbei defend themselves against the attacking Big Mom Pirates.

E835.note: Knowing what he needs to do, Luffy takes the fragments of Carmel's picture and runs towards Big Mom.

E836.note: A flashback begins, showing 63 years ago when Linlin was five years old.

E837.note: Furious at Linlin's actions, Jorul tried to strike down Linlin, only to be mortally wounded by the rampaging child. Linlin's rampage finally stopped when the giants gave her the semla.

E838.note: Linlin cried from Carmel's disappearance, and two people witnessed what really happened.

E839.note: With nowhere else to run, Bege transforms into a fortress known as Big Father.

E840.note: The alliance and the Vinsmoke Family take shelter inside Bege's fortress. However, Big Mom regains her senses and begins attacking the fortress. Bege comes up with a plan to escape.

E841.note: As Caesar carries Bege out of the venue, the Vinsmoke Family covers for them.

E842.note: After Judge is subdued, Niji and Yonji clash with Big Mom. Caesar Clown continues carrying Bege out of the venue but Katakuri stands in his way.

E843.note: As the chateau collapses, the Straw Hats, the Fire Tank Pirates, and the Vinsmoke Family escape from the Big Mom Pirates' clutches.

E844.note: As a crazed Big Mom is causing destruction at Sweet City, Perospero tells her that the Straw Hats had a spare wedding cake in an attempt to stop her from damaging the city any further.

E845.note: As Big Mom unleashes devastating attacks, the Sanji Retrieval Team urges King Baum to keep going as they head into the Seducing Woods.

E846.note: Chopper and Brook reach the Shark Submerge and return to the Thousand Sunny. At the Seducing Woods, Luffy's group continue fleeing from Big Mom on foot.

E847.note: Chopper and Brook return to the Sunny to find the ship occupied by the Big Mom Pirates.

E848.note: After Sanji successfully got on Rabiyan, Pudding helps Luffy's group by preventing the tree homies from hindering them any longer.

E849.note: Luffy's group made it back to the Thousand Sunny. As Luffy battles Katakuri, whose appears to be immune to Armament Haki, the rest prepares the ship for escape.

E850.note: Thanks to Pedro's explosion, Chopper, Brook, and the Thousand Sunny are freed from the candy entrapment.

E851.note: In the Mirro-World, Luffy begins a fierce clash with Katakuri.

E852.note: Sanji, Pudding, and Chiffon arrive at Cacao Island and begin making a new wedding cake with the assistance of the WCI 31, Totto Land's top chefs.

E853.note: Mascarpone and Joscarpone aim and shoot fire arrows through a mirrors to the Sunny from the Mirro-World.

E854.note: The Sunny crew shatters all of the mirrors on the ship, and they make plans with Luffy to reunite at Cacao Island at 1:00 AM.

E855.note: On Whole Cake Island, Count Niwatori cautions Mont-d'Or about the potential of the Straw Hats.

E856.note: After burying Luffy under a mountain of mochi, Katakuri believes he won and calls his chefs, who bring snacks for him to feast on. Katakuri makes a shrine where he can eat in private.

E857.note: After activating Gear Fourth, Luffy manages to deal some damage to Katakuri, having realized the latter has been using his advance Observation Haki to avoid Luffy's attack.

E858.note: With Luffy losing the advantage, Katakuri goes back on the offensive. Luffy retreats when he realizes his Gear Fourth is about to wear off.

E859.note: The chefs at Cacao Island leave the Sweets Factory with the unfinished cake with plans to complete it while out at sea.

E860.note: Oven tells Bege to stand down as he holds Chiffon hostage on Cacao Island, but Bege responds by shooting him.

E861.note: Sanji gets the wedding cake on the Nostra Castello, which runs over Oven as the Fire Tank Pirates and Sanji's group escape out to sea.

E862.note: Luffy and Katakuri resume their battle in the Mirro-World, with Luffy intending to use the battle to strengthen his Haki and defeat Katakuri.

E863.note: Luffy continues focusing during his fight with Katakuri in order to improve his Haki.

E864.note: As Katakuri continues his onslaught, Luffy struggles to raise the level of his Kenbunshoku Haki.

E865.note: Luffy and Katakuri's clash continues. Flampe watches the battle and plans to win Katakuri's favor. Remembering Rayleigh's training, Luffy is beginning to see into the future.

E866.note: Luffy continues to fight with Katakuri. Despite his injuries, he is determined to defeat his enemy and return to the Thousand Sunny.

E867.note: With Big Mom following the Fire Tank Pirates, the group on the Thousand Sunny continues on to Cacao Island.

E868.note: After discovering Flampe's presence in the Mirro-World, Katakuri finds her and confronts her for intruding in his fight.

E869.note: Sanji and Pudding prepare to infiltrate Cacao Island, which Oven has stationed with thousands of his siblings and crewmates.

E870.note: Luffy activates Gear Fourth: Snakeman, a sleeker, less armored form of Bounceman, allows him to rapidly change their direction of his punches.

E871.note: With 1:00 AM drawing closer, Sanji and Pudding reminisce about their experiences together as Sanji prepares to depart.

E872.note: The Sunny crew reaches Cacao Island, and in the Mirro-World, Pekoms tells Luffy he will activate his Sulong form to distract Oven's army.

E873.note: The Vinsmoke Family battles Oven's army on Cacao Island and seeing Sanji and Luffy off, and Brûlée reveals to her crewmates that Luffy defeated Katakuri.

E874.note: The Fire Tank Pirates drop the wedding cake off on Funwari Island, but the Nostra Castello is destroyed while the Big Mom Pirates anxiously await the results of Big Mom eating it.

E875.note: Big Mom eats Sanji's wedding cake, and is overwhelmed with sheer happiness as she enjoys it.

E876.note: The Straw Hats had managed to be saved from death when Wadatsumi switched the Thousand Sunny with the Sun Pirates' ship.

E877.note: Sanji finds out about Pedro's death as the Straw Hats successfully sail out of Totto Land, and as she overhears the news on Cacao Island, Pudding weeps.

E878.note: At Foosha Village and an unnamed island in the New World, Makino and Shanks each remember Luffy's experiences with Shanks and the Red Hair Pirates 12 years ago.

E879.note: As the royals from Dressrosa and Prodence Kingdom sail to Mariejois for the Reverie, they are attacked by pirates, but are saved by Koby and Helmeppo.

E880.note: While the royalty is away for the Reverie, the Peachbeard Pirates raid the Lulucia Kingdom, wanting to steal the country's Heavenly Tribute to the Celestial Dragon.

E881.note: At New Marineford, Sakazuki discovers that Fujitora has gone to Mariejois. He then orders for Ryokugyu to send him away.

E882.note: The nobles, including Shirahoshi from the Ryugu Kingdom, continue to gather for the Reverie. Admiral Fujitora talks with Admiral Ryokugyu on how Dr.

E883.note: King Stelly (Sabo's adopted brother) of the Goa Kingdom (who became king after the mysterious death of the previous king and crown prince) meets Garp and his envoy from Fish-Man Island.

E884.note: The Neptune Family and some other royals reach the top of the Red Line and entered Mariejois. They then arrive at Pangaea Castle and meet with all the participants of the Reverie.

E885.note: Vivi, Rebecca, and Shirahoshi continue with their conversation about Luffy. Sai becomes reacquainted with Rebecca and Leo.

E886.note: Stelly is brought forward to the Empty Throne, which is meant to symbolize that no one man can rule, but Stelly desires to sit on it.

E887.note: Big Mom contacts Kaido, informing him that she will be going to Wano Country to take Luffy's head. Kaido objects, bearing his own grudge against Luffy.

E888.note: After Charlos was taken away, Mjosgard promises to protect Shirahoshi during the Reverie. Meanwhile, Sabo sneaks around Mariejois before going to the Domain of the Gods.

E889.note: As Roswald and Charlos are returning to their home, Sabo and his comrades hold a strategy meeting.

M14.note: One Piece: Stampede is a movie placed around Levely / Reverie. Release-era placement after Levely / before Wano.

E890.note: Cat Viper and the Guardians reach Whitebeard's home island, where Whitebeard and Ace are buried. Cat Viper finds Marco in a hidden village where he works as a doctor.

E891.note: After reading about the Reverie in the newspaper, Luffy's group finds themselves in the perilous sea surrounding the Land of Wano. The crew then encounters a school of giant koi fish.

E892.note: Zoro, Usopp, Franky, and Robin are incognito inside Wano Country. Zoro poses as a ronin, Usopp works as a toad oil merchant, Franky works as a carpenter, and Robin trains as a geisha.

E893.note: While fighting the magistrate's men, Zoro retrieves his swords and demolishes the magistrate's home. Afterwards, Zoro becomes a wanted man.

E894.note: After securing the Sunny, Tama, along with Komachijo and the baboon, who Tama names Hihimaru, takes Luffy to the house where she lives with her master.

E895.note: A bounty hunter group who uses carbonated water-based weapons called the Cidre Guild unsuccessfully attempt to capture the Straw Hats, leaving the Thousand Sunny without any cola fuel.

E896.note: As Luffy fights Cidre as Hancock fights Guarna, who uses a pair of pressurized carbonated hoses as swords.

E897.note: After Luffy dons a kimono and takes the famed blade, Nidai Kitetsu, he and Komachiyo leave Hitetsu to take Tama to a doctor.

E898.note: Zoro takes an interest in Nidai Kitetsu, but Luffy refuses to let him see it before engaging in a fight against Hawkins' men, though Zoro becomes irritated.

E899.note: As Komachiyo flees with Luffy, Zoro, and the ill Tama, Hawkins pursues them with the straw entity. Zoro defends against the straw entity's attacks and eventually defeats it.

E900.note: Luffy, Tsuru and Kiku prepare the medicine for Tama. Okiku also helps to bandage up Zoro after he sustained an injury during the battle with Hawkins.

E901.note: Luffy, Zoro and Kiku, who reveals herself as a samurai, chase after Gazelleman, the gazelle SMILE Fruit Gifter that abducted Tama, but they fail to catch up.

E902.note: Luffy, Zoro, Kiku and Komachiyo enter Bakura Town to rescue Tama after Luffy knocks out the guards with his Conqueror's Haki.

E903.note: Luffy faces against Urashima in a sumo match. Unable to over power Luffy Urashima starts using illegal moves, but Luffy sends him flying him with a Third Gear palm thrust.

E904.note: Luffy, Zoro, and Okiku fight back against the attacking Beast Pirates and their samurai allies.

E905.note: Holdem continues to taunt Luffy's group while holding Otama hostage, threatening to chew her up with Kamijiro.

E906.note: After defeating Holdem, Luffy grabs Otama and flees the scene. During the escape, they encounter Speed and Tama tames her with a kibi dango.

E907.note: An adaptation of the second Romance Dawn one-shot manga, a predecessor to One Piece.

E908.note: As Zoro's group delivers the stolen food cart to Okobore Town a trio of Beast Pirates tries to stop them only to be crushed.

E909.note: As the group heads up the mountain, Luffy is skeptical about seeing ghost.

E910.note: Kin'emon explains to Luffy's group what happened in the past. He recounts stories such as the one of his Lord and Momonosuke's farther, Kozuki Oden.

E911.note: When Kin'emon's group despaired over what has happened to their home, their hope was restored when they met those still loyal to the Koukuki Clan.

E912.note: When Sanji inquires about Ashura Doji, Kin'emon explains that after Oden defeated him, Ashura pledge loyalty to Oden.

E913.note: The drunken Kaido flies above Okobore Town in his dragon form and threatens Shutenmaru and all the people in the town.

E914.note: Right after the destruction of Oden Castle, an enraged Luffy strikes Kaido with an Elephant Gun.

E915.note: Luffy continues his furious battle with Kaido repeatedly attacking with Third Gear.

E916.note: After his defeat, Luffy is sent to a prison in the Udon region to have his spirit broken to make him join Kaido's crew.

E917.note: Perona learns from a newspaper that Gecko Moria is alive and prepares to leave Kuraigana Island to search for him.

E918.note: Dogstorm clashes with Ashura, who refuses to aid in Kin'emon's operation. Meanwhile, Sanji, under the guise of a soba cook, and the others seek out recruits.

E919.note: Nami and Shinobu finish their current objective in the Flower Capital and escape via kite as Sanji's soba stand is growing more popular.

E920.note: Sanji tangles with some yakuza members from the Kyoshiro Family who disrupt his soba business.

E921.note: At the Prison Mine, Luffy is approached by Caribou who ask for food, but an annoyed Luffy tells him to go away.

E922.note: Zoro goes to a gambling house with a sycophant named Tonoyasu. After Zoro exposes the house of cheating, a brawl breaks out.

E923.note: Once arriving in Ebisu Town, Zoro discovers that it's just as poor as Okobore Town, but despite that, all the townsfolk laugh despite their misery.

E924.note: King transforms into a pteranodon with his Dragon-Dragon Fruit, Ancient Type form: Pteranodon powers and flies to the waterfall while the Big Mom Pirates ascend.

E925.note: As he confronts Page One, Sanji puts on his Raid Suit in order to disguise himself.

E926.note: Off the coast of the Land of Wano, the Big Mom Pirates try to figure out their next course of actions.

E927.note: At Kuri Beach, Chopper gives the amnesiac Big Mom a fake name, Olin. Back inside the shogun's castle, Orochi warns his guests about the return of the Akazaya Nine.

E928.note: Orochi takes his rage out on Komurasaki as Robin protects Toko.

E929.note: In the Ringo region, Kanjuro meets up with Shinobu's group at the Northern Cemetery.

E930.note: Raizo manages to steal some keys from the Prisoner Mines executive tower.

E931.note: Luffy and Hyougoro fail to escape and are quickly surrounded by the Beast Pirates. They manage to defeat some of the soldiers but are detained by Daifugo and Babanuki.

E932.note: At the Prisoner Mines, the Sumo Inferno begins, but Luffy easily knocks out his opponents with Conquerors Haki.

E933.note: The Sumo Inferno continues and Queen sends in the Gifters Batman and Gazelleman after Luffy defeats the Pleasures, while Luffy tries to improve his Armament Haki to the advance stage.

E934.note: Due to a leak, those allied with the Kozuki Family are arrested in the Flower Capital. Sanji's group returns to Ebisu Town where he is beaten for intruding in the bathhouse.

E935.note: Luffy and Hyogoro are confronted by Alpacaman and Madilloman, a armadillo SMILE Fruit Gifter.

E936.note: After Hiyori reveals her identity to Zoro, she explains that while her mother sent Momonosuke, Kanjuro, Raizo and Kikunojo twenty years into the future.

E937.note: Luffy continues to try to learn how to advance his Armament Haki, which Hyogoro says it referred to as Ryuo in Wano, through fighting in the Sumo Inferno.

E938.note: After being capture, Tonoyasu claims to be the infamous thief, Ushimitzu Kozo. When Tonoyasu bandana falls off, Fukurokuju recognizes him.

E939.note: Toko and Zoro's group make their way to the Flower Capital. Tonoyasu is revealed to be Shimotsuki Yasuie the former daimyo of Hakumai.

E940.note: As Ashura searches for Holdem for burning down his mountain base he comes across the broadcast of Yasuie before Kin'emon and Dogstorm arrive.

E941.note: As the citizens of Ebisu Town grieve over Yasuie's death, Shinobu and Hiyori reveals more information about the SMILE fruits.

E942.note: Zoro and Sanji manage to save Toko.

E943.note: Zoro clashes with Kyoshiro and Sanji clashes with Drake while Usopp takes Toko to safety.

E944.note: After the skirmish inside the Flower Capital, Kin'emon confesses to Ashura that he provoked Holdem into attacking Mt. Atama.

E945.note: Big Mom subdues Queen when the latter tries to fight her. During the scuffle, Kid and Killer are saved from drowning and Kawamatsu's cell is broken apart.

E946.note: Luffy attempts to defend against Big Mom's attack, but she sends him and Hyogoro crashing into a wall. They both survive, but Hyogoro is injured.

E947.note: Right after Big Mom regains her memories, she suddenly falls asleep. Queen quickly orders his men to restrain Big Mom and escorts her to Onigashima.

E948.note: Raizo retrieves the keys to Kawamatsu's cell as well as his sword, Soto Moto, but is confronted by Solitaire, though he is able to escape.

E949.note: Babanuki's men continue firing Plague Rounds at the prisoners, with ehe bullet wounds form black skull marks, spreading green blotches all over the body.

E950.note: After Udon is conquered, a collapsed Luffy asks Kid if he wants join the fight against Kaido, but Kid rejects his offer, having lost trust in alliances after Hawkins'.

E951.note: Zoro and Hiyori have an encounter with the ninja Fujin and several ninjas that serve Orochi.

E952.note: Kanjuro visits Ebisu Town where he receives permission to bury Yasuie's body in Kuri as the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance continues with their preparations for the Fire Festival.

E953.note: At Onigashima, Kaido and Big Mom continue with their clash. Queen contacts Udon, and Babanuki, having been tamed by Tama, gives Queen a false report.

E954.note: Kawamatsu rekindles his bond with Hiyori whilst Zoro chases after Gyukimaru. Kawamatsu explains that in Ringo, people are given a sword at birth which they keep for their entire life.

E955.note: At the secret place where Kawamatsu stored weapons, Hiyori explains to Zoro that before he died, Oden gave his two swords to his children, Ame No Hebakiri to Momonosuke.

E956.note: Hiyori parts ways with Zoro and Kawamatsu, and the two later inform Momonosuke and the Akazaya Nine with him of her survival.

E957.note: After the Reverie concludes, Neptune's family returns to Fish-Man Island accompanied by Garp, where he laments the tense and violent arguments that occurred during the Reverie.

E958.note: At the new Marine Headquarters, the Marine officers hold a meeting where Sengoku explains the Rock Pirates and the history concerning this crew.

E959.note: Orochi and his men heads to Onigashima after departing from Tabu Port on the day of the Fire Festival.

E960.note: It's revealed that Orochi had the Beast Pirates bomb the Thousand Sunny and destroy bridges to cut off routes to Tokage Port.

E961.note: The Mountain God continues its rampage around town in search of its baby. Upon hearing that the Mountain God swallowed Tsurujo, Kin'emon decides to battle it head on.

E962.note: Oden defeats Ashura Doji and his bandits and earns the title of the Daimyo of Kuri with Sukiyaki repealing his disavowal and accepts him as his son again.

E963.note: Kozuki Oden's dreams to leave Wano leads him directly to Whitebeard after he learns of the pirate's arrival. After a brief battle, Oden begs Whitebeard to let him join his crew.

E964.note: Toki tends to Oden after he rescued her from the kidnappers.

E965.note: Orochi became Oden's proxy as he was at sea, but the other daimyos suspect him because he was a member of the Kurozumi Family.

E966.note: After three days of battle between the Roger Pirates and the Whitebeard Pirates, a ceasefire was called and on the fourth day the two crews began a gift exchange.

E967.note: While enjoying time with the crew, Oden is shocked to learn of Roger's illness and he has about a year to live. The Roger Pirates travel the Grand Line as they visit several islands.

E968.note: Oden's adventure with the Roger Pirates led him back to his homeland of the Land of Wano, since there was a Road Poneglyph there.

E969.note: After attaining everything and becoming the King of the Pirates, Roger disbands his crew.

E970.note: Oden charges to the Flower Capital to confront Orochi, cutting down anyone who tries to stop him.

E971.note: During the second year of Oden's return the Beast Pirates and Gecko Pirates clash against each other as someone vandalizes Ryuma's grave.

E972.note: Shinobu helps Oden and the Akazaya Nine in their battle as King and Queen join the fray to confront Oden's retainers.

E973.note: Although Orochi initially scoffs at Oden's suggestion, Kaidou accepts the challenge, agreeing to release the retainers if they survive one hour in the pot.

E974.note: Fukurokuju confronts Shinobu for telling everyone the truth, but Shinobu denounces the Orochi Oniwabanshu and pledges loyalty to the Akazaya Nine.

E975.note: News of Oden's death spread to Toki, who reflected on her final moments with Oden.

E976.note: Orochi frustration grows over the fact Oden's retainers had not been caught. Meanwhile, Oden's remaining followers were devastated by the deaths.

E977.note: No allies have to come to aid the raid on Onigashima so the Akazaya Nine jump on a rowboat to go to Onigashima. Kikunojo deduces that there must be a traitor amongst them.

E978.note: A naval battle between the Beast Pirates and the alliance ensues.

E979.note: After destroying the Beast Pirates' ships, Kyoshiro removes his fake pompadour and reveals his real identity as Denjiro.

E980.note: As Kawamatsu frees Shinobu from the drawn snakes, the Straw Hats become enraged from learning of Kanjuro's betrayal.

E981.note: The Straw Hats are happy to welcome Jimbei into their crew.

E982.note: The allies continue to discuss their plans on getting past the front gate when they notice the Straw Hats are missing.

E983.note: Luffy and Kid race to Onigashima, with Jimbei controlling the Thousand Sunny. Meanwhile, Raizo briefs the plan.

E984.note: Although Orochi believes that the Akazaya Nine have been stopped, the raid on Onigashima officially begins.

E985.note: As the Tobi Roppo go to see Kaido, it revealed that King was the one who summoned them in order to aid Kaido with a situation.

E986.note: Remembering Tama's joy from oshiruko for the first time in Okobore Town, Luffy explains how wasteful the partygoers were being.

E987.note: Kid confronts Apoo about his betrayal, but Apoo fights back as Queen, shocked that Kid has also escaped from the prisoner mine, gives his men permission to kill the intruders.

E988.note: Although Luffy attempts to follow the plan, Kid and Killer continue to wreak havoc on enemy units. Meanwhile, at the eastern mountain path, Kin'emon's forces split into two groups.

E989.note: Big Mom recognizes Chopper as a Straw Hat, forcing Chopper and Usopp attempt to flee in Brachio Tank V with Big Mom chasing after them.

E990.note: A mysterious masked figure runs through the halls, excited to meet someone while being chased by the Beast Pirates who see them.

E991.note: Angry that the Big Mom Pirates' ship was knocked down the waterfall twice, Perospero rides to Onigashima on his candy slug.

E992.note: Deciding to trust Yamato, Luffy agrees to hear him out as Pade One and the Beast Pirates attempt to locate them. Orochi prepares to execute Momonosuke to damage the enemy morale.

E993.note: Yamato tells Luffy how Ace came to Wano for Kaido's head and mentioned Luffy a lot. Yamato also admits to have wanted to join Ace's crew but couldn't.

E994.note: The alliance in the Performance Floor are shocked by Big Mom's appearance and her capture of Nami's group.

E995.note: To Hyogoro's shock and disgust, Orochi's subordinates decide to join Kaido's side and abandon their shogun.

E996.note: As he falls, Kaidou realizes that the Akazaya Nine used Ryuo to pierce through his body. He crashes into the ground below.

E997.note: War ensues in Onigashima. Big Mom and Kaido assume that the raid would be easily defeated, as Luffy only had a few hundred troops.

E998.note: Shinobu tries to rescue Momonosuke but is caught by King, who prepares to kill Momonosuke until Sanji rescues him in his Raid Suit and tosses him to Shinobu.

E999.note: While everyone is surprised by Franky running over Big Mom, Luffy prepares to go to the roof. Usopp and Chopper try to escape from Numbers members, Jaki, Goki and Juki but to no avail.

E1000.note: Big Mom rolls out of the castle and down the entrance steps, being spotted by Perospero.

E1001.note: Drake watches the alliance take on the Beast Pirates and thinks back to Koby telling him how Luffy draws people in.

E1002.note: Before Luffy can answer, Zoro attacks Drake and tells Luffy not to trust him with Franky and Jimbei agreeing as well.

E1003.note: The samurai shot by Queen continue to freeze over and turn into onis, attacking their own comrades who turn into demons as well.

E1004.note: As Queen continues firing Plague Ronuns into the crowd, King orders Bao Huang to locate Momonosuke, who along with Shinobu, is still being chased by Yamato.

E1005.note: The slash that the Akazaya Nine inflicted on Kaido draws blood the scars that Oden made, as collapses from the injury.

E1006.note: Shinobu and Momonosuke are chased by Yamato into the right brain tower. Shinobu uses a falling piece of paper to divert Yamato away from him.

E1007.note: As the Ice Oni virus spreads throughout the live floor, Omasa considers killing himself to prevent himself from becoming an oni, but Hyougoro dissuades him.

E1008.note: Marco battles with Big Mom, with his blue phoenix flames clashing with Prometheus' flames.

E1009.note: Still in grief over Pedro's death, Carrot becomes furious at the sight of Perospero. Wanda consoles her, stating that Pedro took his turn as he promised and did not die with regrets.

E1010.note: Law finds a Poneglyph while thinking back to when Corazon told him about D. and telling Robin his true name. He continues to look for the Road Poneglyph in order to learn D.

E1011.note: Sanji splits away from the group to rescue the harassed woman.

E1012.note: With Chopper, discovering that fire can be used against the Ice Oni virus, Marco uses his phoenix flames to slowdown the Ice Oni's spread on its victims.

E1013.note: Sanji tries to escape Black Maria only to be captured by her again in her jorougumo-like Human-Beast Form of her Spider-Spider Fruit, form: Rosamygale Grauvogeli.

E1014.note: The samurai defend Chopper as he makes the antidote and Marco restores more Ice Oni victims. Queen order the Beasts Pirates, who are reluctant at first, to attack Marco to no avail.

E1015.note: Yamato shows Momonosuke his father's journal and tells him how she found it.

E1016.note: Kaido attacks Luffy, who barely dodges and is pleased, especially after seeing the shadows of those who were capable of fighting him behind Luffy.

E1017.note: Kaido prepares a counterattack, but Luffy strikes Kaido's stomach with Kong Gun, Kid crushes him with his fists using Punk Vise, and Law pummels him with rocks using Tact.

E1018.note: Luffy continues to hit Kaido with Kong Gatling and knock him into the roof with everybody in the Skull Dome noticing the vibrations, astounding Big Mom and the Worst Generation.

E1019.note: On Onigashima, Speed starts distributing kibi dangos to the Gifters saying they're medicine created by Queen, apparently granting added strength.

E1020.note: Black Maria's women beat Sanji until he's knocked out and he wakes up to see Black Maria has crucified him with webs. Maria orders Sanji to call for Robin using a surveillance unit.

E1021.note: Robin strikes down Black Maria as Brook frees Sanji by freezing and shattering the webs. As Robin faces Black Maria, she thanks Sanji for relying on her as he escapes.

E1022.note: On the live floor of Skull Dome, Chopper works on the antidote but starts to run out of stamina. Still, he pushes through, using the last of his strength.

E1023.note: After taking the antidote, Chopper is cured, and the ice crumbles off his skin. Marco struggles against King and Queen, who attack him from two angles. He is sent crashing into a wall.

E1024.note: In the Right Brain Tower, Yamato and Shinobu defend Momonosuke from enemy Beast Pirates, but Momonosuke becomes frustrated by his inability to fend for himself.

E1025.note: The Akazaya Nine fight off the Kanjuro-controlled Oden, but Oden reveals and lights an explosive vest.

E1026.note: Orochi and Fukurokuju continue to set fire to the castle, as they prepare to go to the explosive storage.

E1027.note: Big Mom falls into the ocean below and starts to drown, but her homies are unable to go rescue her.

E1028.note: Luffy's declaration angers Kaido. Meanwhile, Big Mom takes out a fragment of her soul and gives it to the thundercloud above, creating a new homie to replace Zeus.

E1029.note: While at sea the Straw Hat Pirates listen to the hit song 'New Genesis'. Meanwhile, Uta stands on a cliff, staring out into the ocean and thinking about her childhood.

E1030.note: Luffy and Uta spend their time competing with each other.

M15.note: One Piece Film: Red is a movie placed around Uta's Past. Watch after episodes 1029-1030 (Uta's Past tie-in).

E1031.note: When Big Mom's new homie hit Kid and Killer with its attack, Kid's imprisonment of Zeus collapsed, finally freeing him.

E1032.note: The grouped Akazaya head to Momonosuke to protect him from Kanjuro. Kikunojo states that the pain from her arm was far less than the pain of when Izo left.

E1033.note: Sanji carries and protects Zoro as he heals, angry that he had to carry someone with a lower bounty than him. He remembers how this happened.

E1034.note: Kaido watches as Luffy falls into the ocean, remarking that though Luffy coated himself in Conqueror's Haki, he was still sloppy and unable to wield it perfectly.

E1035.note: As Momonosuke continues to react to the mysterious voice, Shinobu begins to panic, allowing Kin'emon and Kikunojo to find them.

E1036.note: As Luffy sinks into the ocean, Chopper continues to battle Queen, but is demotivated from his captain's defeat.

E1037.note: As Luffy sinks into the sea, Momonosuke uses a Mary to inform everybody located on Onigashima of his survival, and to declare that Luffy will defeat Kaido.

E1038.note: The Heart Pirates find a sinking Luffy, bringing him to the surface on their submarine. They begin trying to treat him, forcing the water he's swallowed out of his stomach.

E1039.note: Across Onigashima, the Gifters who have eaten Tama's dango begin to switch sides, attacking former allies.

E1040.note: As Speed carries Tama, Nami, and Usopp through Onigashima, with Gazelleman and Daifugo following close behind in order to recover Komachiyo, defending the trio along the way.

E1041.note: Kaido and Yamato clash on the Onigashima rooftop, as Yamato attempts to cut ties with her father.

E1042.note: Yamato and Kaido continue to duel on the rooftop, with Yamato officially refusing Kaido's offer to become Shogun.

E1043.note: As Robin runs from Black Maria's Gifters, she is joined by Brook, who relieves that she was not tricked by the Illusion Mist.

E1044.note: Black Maria traps Robin's enlarged form in her web, and starts to sadistically beat Robin with her brass knuckles.

E1045.note: A Mary reports Black Maria defeat to CP0.

E1046.note: King intercepts Chopper's group which is carrying Zoro. Sanji sees this and tries to defend against King but is shot by Queen's laser.

E1047.note: As the castle is slowly being engulfed by fire, many Beast Pirates quickly move towards the Performance Floor where Sanji, Zoro, King and Queen are fighting, Hyougoro.

E1048.note: Luffy rides on Momonusuke's back and they start flying towards Kaido. However, being afraid of heights and having realized that they have been flying, Momonosuke comes crashing down.

E1049.note: Luffy urges Momonosuke to fly and grows impatient, but the young master is crippled by his fear of heights. Meanwhile, Yamato keeps Kaido busy and the two continue to exchange blows.

E1050.note: After sending Kaido flying across the rooftop, Luffy thanks Yamato for holding back Kaido and Yamato expresses her relief that Luffy has finally arrived.

E1051.note: Luffy's proud declaration from the Skull Dome rooftop inspires the remaining fighters of the alliance to continue fighting.

E1052.note: Momonosuke and Yamato fly away from the island to try to move it back.

E1053.note: Momonosuke attempts to make Flame Clouds in order to move Onigashima, but he struggles to create them as the clouds immediately disintegrate.

E1054.note: Killer continues his battle against Hawkins, but the battle is not in his favor.

E1055.note: After Queen's blade shatters upon contact with Sanji's neck, Sanji realizes that he may have scientific enhancements just like his siblings.

E1056.note: As Momonosuke attempts to create the flame clouds needed to stop Onigashima, Yamato runs to the armory to secure the explosives.

E1057.note: Apoo tries to convince Drake to team up against Kaido after the emperor defeats Luffy, stating Kaido has never been so weakened before.

E1058.note: An angered Big Mom promises to inflict unspeakable pain towards Law and Kid.

E1059.note: Kazenbo continues to make its way down to the Armory as the he Beast Pirates and samurai try in vain to stop its descent.

E1060.note: Zoro struggles against King as Emma constantly drains his Haki.

E1061.note: Sanji continues his battle against Queen. After Sanji destroys his Raid Suit, Queen decides to continue on with the battle.

E1062.note: With his swords now infused with both Armament and Conqueror's Haki, emanating the black lightning and a green smoke-like aura, Zoro.

E1063.note: Zoro loses consciousness after defeating King.

E1064.note: While the final battle between Luffy and Kaido is taking place at the rooftop of the Skull Dome.

E1065.note: On the third floor, Raizo and Fukurokuju remain at a standstill with neither one of them releasing their Paralysis Jutsu despite both catching on fire.

E1066.note: As Momonosuke continues to pull Onigashima away from the Flower Capital, he is momentarily distracted when he suddenly hears someone calling out to him.

E1067.note: The standoff between Raizo and Fukurokuju continues as Raizo is ablaze and Fukurokuju is eventually completely set alight.

E1068.note: Zunesha appears at Onigashima to aid the battle, but Momonosuke is unsure of whether to continue the battle.

E1069.note: In the connected hallway, Fukurokuju completely catches fire and collapses to the flames. Raizo makes his final remarks to him before he succumbs to his injuries.

E1070.note: Taking a critical strike from Kaido's kanabo, Luffy is defeated, deflating out of his Fourth Gear form and collapses.

E1071.note: Zunesha reveals to Momonosuke and Yamato that Joy Boy has returned. On the top of the Skull Dome, Luffy finishes his transformation, having fully awakened his Devil Fruit maximum power.

E1072.note: With their final battle now resumed, Kaido states that he was sure Luffy had died, to which the latter agrees.

E1073.note: Kaido tells Luffy that Devil Fruit Awakening occurs when one's mind and body catch up to their powers.

E1074.note: Momonosuke struggles and fails to produce Flame Clouds to sustain Onigashima. He begins to doubt himself, but he remembers his final interaction with his mother, he regains his resolve.

E1075.note: Momonosuke is tasked with moving Onigashima out of the way of Luffy's final powerful attack. However, he feels he is unable to move the island in time.

E1076.note: As he clashes with Luffy, Kaido's past is revealed.

E1077.note: As Luffy falls asleep and Yamato catches him, Onigashima lands safely near the Flower Capital.

E1078.note: In the Flower Capital, Kyoshiro, revealing himself to be Denjiro, announces the return of the Akazya Nine.

E1079.note: In Mary Geoise, the Five Elders learn of the defeat of two Emperors, as well as the liberation of the Land of Wano. They recognize the return of Nika in the form of Luffy.

E1080.note: News of Big Mom and Kaido's defeat spread internationally, but the Five Elders attempt to censor Luffy's wanted poster depicting him as Nika and remove the "D. " from his name.

E1081.note: The Red Hair Pirates approach the borders of the Land of Wano.

E1082.note: As the Straw Hat Pirates and Heart Pirates continue to participate in the Flower Capital's festivities.

E1083.note: Kin'emon and Kikunojo visit Raizo and Shinobu, in critical condition due to dehydration inflicted by Ryokugyu.

E1084.note: Before their departure from the Land Wano, Luffy and the Akazaya Nine (minus Kin'emon) and Shinobu visit the mountain where Kuri Castle once stood.

E1085.note: Wano Country starts to thrive after Orochi is deposed and Kaido is defeated. The tale of the Raid on Onigashima is narrated on stage in front of an audience.

E1086.note: As the Straw Hats leave Wano, Nami beats up Luffy and locks him in a cage for going down the waterfall. The crew then find out their new bounties.

E1087.note: As Marco departs the Red Hair Pirates ship taking him to Whitebeard's homeland Sphinx, he remembers leaving the land of Wano.

E1088.note: Joined by Rayleigh and Shakky (whose full name is revealed to be Shakuyaku, and is the former captain of the Kuja Pirates and former Empress of Amazon Lily two generations prior).

E1089.note: After leaving Wano, the Straw Hats pursue the Final Road Poneglyph, vital to Luffy's dream of becoming the King of the Pirates.

E1090.note: Luffy, Chopper, and the girl, who reveals herself as Jewelry Bonney, are rescued by Jimbei.

E1091.note: Lilith, a satellite of Vegapunk, attacks the Thousand Sunny, but is stopped by Shaka, another satellite of Vegapunk, who identifies Zoro and Robin.

E1092.note: A CP0 unit that resembles Bartholomew Kuma is dispatched to Egghead to eliminate Dr. Vegapunk.

E1093.note: After an assault by the Blackbeard Pirates, the Heart Pirates flee and dock on a nearby island. Trafalgar Law is confronted and attacked by Doc Q.

E1094.note: Luffy, Chopper, Jimbei, and Bonney evade the Pacifista after Bonney uses her Devil Fruit powers to confuse it.

E1095.note: The Straw Hats fall into Lilith's trap and are forced to face S-Shark, who wields multiple powers. Sanji, Robin, and Usopp's attacks fail against it.

E1096.note: Shaka discusses his theory with Sanji's group regarding an ancient kingdom connected to the Void Century.

E1097.note: Shaka tells Sanji's group about how the giants saved Ohara's books 22 years ago when Dr. Vegapunk met up with Dragon. He also confirms to Robin the identity of their leader.

E1098.note: Luffy's group questions Vegapunk about the Iron Giant, a legendary robot linked to an ancient power source.

E1099.note: As Lucci's group launches their invasion of Egghead, Shaka begins the preparations to deal with it. However, Atlas ignores orders and is damaged when attempting to fight Lucci.

E1100.note: Sakazuki learns that the Straw Hats are on Egghead and are aligning with Vegapunk. He sends Kizaru to support Lucci and the CP0 agents.

E1101.note: The battle between Luffy and Lucci continues, with Luffy overpowering Lucci.

E1102.note: Luffy, Bonney, and Jimbei make it to Vegapunk's lab, where Atlas is tended to. With the defeat of Sentomaru, CP0 regains control of the Seraphims.

E1103.note: Kaku and Zoro engage in a battle after Kaku fails to destroy the Thousand Sunny. Bonney pursues Dr. Vegapunk, demanding answers about Kuma. The Kid Pirates arrive at Elbaf.

E1104.note: Bonney discovers a glowing paw, realizing it contains Kuma's memories stored before he became a cyborg. Vegapunk tries to deceive her but fails.

E1105.note: Stussy reveals herself to be a spy for Vegapunk. Zoro briefly battles S-Hawk until Edison arrives and regains control over the Seraphims.

E1106.note: Sentomaru sends numerous Pacifista Mark III units to handle World Government officials.

E1107.note: Bonney delves into her father's memories, witnessing his childhood trauma.

E1108.note: As Usopp's group faces off against S-Snake, Zoro and Luffy are trapped in their own battle against S-Bear and S-Hawk.

E1109.note: Zoro and Luffy battle S-Hawk and S-Bear, aided by Lucci and Kaku after negotiations for a temporary truce.

E1110.note: Luffy, Zoro, Lucci, and Kaku battle S-Bear and S-Hawk, whose Lunarian abilities resist attacks. Zoro and Shaka discover their weakness: vulnerability when their flames are extinguished.

E1111.note: Kizaru's ship heads to Egghead with Saint Jaygarcia Saturn on board. Franky fails to stop S-Snake from destroying Pythagoras.

E1112.note: Hours earlier, York planned a covert attack using the Seraphim, sparing herself, Vegapunk, and Cipher Pol hostages in the Labophase.

E1113.note: Perona breaks into the Blackbeard Pirates' prison to rescue her captain, Gecko Moria, and frees Koby, who then helps free other prisoners.

E1114.note: Koby finds himself cornered by a mob of pirates. When they attempt to shoot him, their guns fire flowers instead of bullets due to Hibari's use of Flower Bullets.

E1115.note: In the past, Blackbeard and Kuzan initially clashed but resolved their conflict over drinks, with Kuzan joining Blackbeard's crew. In the present, Garp battles Kuzan.

E1116.note: Tsuru and Sengoku learn of Vice Admiral T Bone's murder by a poor civilian seeking bounty money.

E1117.note: Sabo, Dragon, and Ivankov celebrate their success at the Reverie, including revolutions in eight nations and attacks on World Government suppliers.

E1118.note: After obtaining the keys to the slaves' collars and meeting Bonney, Sabo heads to find the Lost Chamber.

E1119.note: Imu tells Cobra about the truth of the Will of D. and the Poneglyphs and demands to know Lili's true name. Knowing they will kill him anyway, Cobra reveals her true name is Nefertari D.

E1120.note: After fleeing from Mary Geoise, Vivi and Wapol stow away to the Eigis Kingdom. Wapol contacts Morgans but withholds details about the Empty Throne Room.

E1121.note: At the Marine ship scrapyard, Commodore Brannew recounts how Garp used battleships as punching bags in his youth, a practice later adopted by Kuzan.

E1122.note: In the past, Garp taught Marine recruits about the value of protecting the future by prioritizing a baby over an old man.

E1123.note: The World Shakes! The Straw Hats' Hostage Situation belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1124.note: Luffy communicates with the Five Elders, confirming his alliance with Dr. Vegapunk and demanding the fleet’s withdrawal for York's safety.

E1125.note: The battle escalates as the Marines attack the Pacifistas, but the Sea Beast weapons retaliate, causing Vice Admiral intervention. Dr.

E1126.note: Looming Despair! Admiral Kizaru's Depressing Mission belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1127.note: Luffy vs. Kizaru! A Fierce Kaleidoscopic Battle belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1128.note: The Nightmare Strikes - Godhead of Science & Defense, St. Saturn belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1129.note: Bonney gets captured by St. Saturn, whose enigmatic power keeps Sanji and Franky from saving her. In St. Saturn's clenched fist, she recalls the memories of her father, Kuma.

E1130.note: The human hunt begins and the Celestial Dragons start chasing locals of God Valley and slaves for prizes.

E1131.note: Kuma and Ginny live peacefully in the Sorbet Kingdom eight years after the God Valley Incident. However, their new king initiates a policy of enslaving the poor.

E1132.note: Two years after Ginny’s abduction, the Revolutionary Army suddenly receives a call from her.

E1133.note: By fighting off King Bekori who is supported by the World Government, Kuma is put on the wanted list and decides to travel around the world in search of a cure for Bonney's disease.

E1134.note: Taking advantage of Kuma's love for his daughter Bonney, the World Government imposes three harsh conditions on him in exchange for her treatment.

E1135.note: Bonney has been waiting for a letter from her father Kuma who promised to write to her. But with no letters in sight, she turns nine and one day realizes her disease is cured.

E1136.note: Bonney sails out to sea in search of her father and rises to prominence.

E1137.note: After Bonney comes out of the memory bubble, Vegapunk gives her the gift Kuma had for her tenth birthday.

E1138.note: After Saturn was knocked away Kuma and Bonney embrace, as Vegapunk wonders why Kuma is still moving due to a failsafe that should have stopped him earlier.

E1141.note: Reliable Reinforcements! Dorry and Brogy Arrive! belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1142.note: Come In, World: Vegapunk's Message belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1143.note: Vegapunk's Secret Plan: A Tense Worldwide Broadcast belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1144.note: After the other Elders arrive at Egghead in their transformed states, they separate to deal with the opposition.

E1145.note: After Mars breaks into Labophase and Jinbe dragged Zoro away, Mars gets the information from Lucci, allowing him to find York.

E1146.note: Mars frees York and they begin searching for the source of Vegapunk's broadcast, as Luffy, Dorry, and Brogy continue their rush to the shore, Peter and Warcury at their backs.

E1147.note: A Stunning Conclusion: Vegapunk's Great Prediction belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1148.note: The Lost History - Joyboy, the First Pirate belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1149.note: The Void Century - A Revelation About a Sinking World belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1150.note: Get the Ship Moving! The Iron Giant Activates belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1151.note: Her and Her Father's Dream! Bonney's Free Future belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1152.note: Her Father and Mother's Legacy! Bonney's Nika Punch belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1153.note: The Upheaval of an Era! The Color of the Supreme King That Leads Luffy belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1154.note: The Truth Behind the Secret Plan - Vegapunk Claims Victory belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1155.note: The Promised Horizon - Off to the Long-Awaited Elbaph! belongs to Egghead in the Final Saga timeline.

E1156.note: After leaving Egghead, the Straw Hats and the Giant Pirate Crew celebrate their reunion with a big banquet.

E1157.note: Nami in a Fix! An Adventure in Block Kingdom belongs to Elbaf in the Final Saga timeline.

E1158.note: A Quest in the Land of Mystery! The Secret of the Sun God belongs to Elbaf in the Final Saga timeline.

E1159.note: Destroy the Miniature Garden - Escape Block Kingdom! belongs to Elbaf in the Final Saga timeline.

E1160.note: An Encounter on a Snowfield - Loki, the Accursed Prince belongs to Elbaf in the Final Saga timeline.
